\"components/xpath\" }\n   228→\n   229→# RSA key generation could be very slow without compilation\n   230→# optimizations, in development mode. Without optimizations, WPT might\n   231→# consider RSA key generation tests fail due to timeout.\n   232→# Building with higher optimization levels can speed up the key\n   233→# generation process to avoid false negative result in those tests.\n   234→# More details: <https://docs.rs/rsa/0.9.8/rsa/index.html#example>\n   235→[profile.dev.package.num-bigint-dig]\n   236→opt-level = 3\n   237→\n   238→# Force tikv-jemalloc-sys to build with at least -O1.\n   239→# This can help with build environments that force _FORTIFY_SOURCE, such as Nix.\n   240→# More details: <https://github.com/tikv/jemallocator/issues/108>\n   241→[profile.dev.package.tikv-jemalloc-sys]\n   242→opt-level = 1\n   243→\n   244→[profile.release]\n   245→opt-level = 3\n   246→debug-assertions = true\n   247→\n   248→# Disk-space is limited in CI, so we disabled features that are not needed for code coverage.\n   249→# Locally using the default `dev` profile is perfectly fine.\n   250→[profile.coverage]\n   251→inherits = \"dev\"\n   252→incremental = false\n   253→debug = false\n   254→\n   255→# A profile between `dev` and `release` which aims to offer a compromise between\n   256→# fast incremental rebuilds and runtime speed.\n   257→[profile.medium]\n   258→inherits = \"release\"\n   259→opt-level = 2\n   260→incremental = true\n   261→debug = \"line-tables-only\"\n   262→\n   263→[profile.production]\n   264→inherits = \"release\"\n   265→debug-assertions = false\n   266→lto = true\n   267→codegen-units = 1\n   268→opt-level = \"s\"\n   269→\n   270→[profile.production-stripped]\n   271→inherits = \"production\"\n   272→strip = true\n   273→\n   274→[profile.profiling]\n   275→inherits = \"release\"\n   276→debug-assertions = false\n   277→debug = true\n   278→lto = \"thin\"\n   279→codegen-units = 1\n   280→\n   281→[patch.crates-io]\n   282→# If you need to temporarily test Servo with a local fork of some upstream\n   283→# crate, add that here. Use the form:\n   284→#\n   285→#     <crate> = { path = \"/path/to/local/checkout\" }\n   286→#\n   287→# For html5ever:\n   288→#\n   289→# html5ever = { path = \"../html5ever/html5ever\" }\n   290→# markup5ever = { path = \"../html5ever/markup5ever\" }\n   291→# web_atoms = { path = \"../html5ever/web_atoms\" }\n   292→# xml5ever = { path = \"../html5ever/xml5ever\" }\n   293→# tendril = { path = \"../html5ever/tendril\" }\n   294→#\n   295→# Or for Stylo:\n   296→#\n   297→# [patch.\"https://github.com/servo/stylo\"]\n   298→# selectors = { path = \"../stylo/selectors\" }\n   299→# servo_arc = { path = \"../stylo/servo_arc\" }\n   300→# stylo = { path = \"../stylo/style\" }\n   301→# stylo_atoms = { path = \"../stylo/stylo_atoms\" }\n   302→# stylo_config = { path = \"../stylo/stylo_config\" }\n   303→# stylo_dom = { path = \"../stylo/stylo_dom\" }\n   304→# stylo_malloc_size_of = { path = \"../stylo/malloc_size_of\" }\n   305→# stylo_traits = { path = \"../stylo/style_traits\" }\n   306→#\n   307→# Or for WebRender:\n   308→#\n   309→# [patch.\"https://github.com/servo/webrender\"]\n   310→# webrender = { path = \"../webrender/webrender\" }\n   311→# webrender_api = { path = \"../webrender/webrender_api\" }\n   312→# wr_malloc_size_of = { path = \"../webrender/wr_malloc_size_of\" }\n   313→#\n   314→# Or for another Git dependency:\n   315→#\n   316→# [patch.\"https://github.com/servo/<repository>\"]\n   317→# <crate> = { path = \"/path/to/local/checkout\" }\n   318→#\n   319→# [patch.\"https://github.com/servo/rust-content-security-policy\"]\n   320→# content-security-policy = { path = \"../rust-content-security-policy\" }\n   321→#\n   322→# Or for mozjs:\n   323→#\n   324→# mozjs = { path = \"../mozjs/mozjs\" }\n   325→# mozjs_sys = { path = \"../mozjs/mozjs-sys\" }\n   326→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this\n * file, You can obtain one at https://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/. */\n\nuse cssparser::{Parser, ParserInput};\nuse euclid::{Scale, Size2D};\nuse servo_arc::Arc;\nuse style::applicable_declarations::CascadePriority;\nuse style::context::QuirksMode;\nuse style::custom_properties::{\n    ComputedCustomProperties, CustomPropertiesBuilder, DeferFontRelativeCustomPropertyResolution,\n    Name, SpecifiedValue,\n};\nuse style::dom::DummyAttributeProvider;\nuse style::font_metrics::FontMetrics;\nuse style::media_queries::{Device, MediaType};\nuse style::properties::style_structs::Font;\nuse style::properties::{ComputedValues, CustomDeclaration, CustomDeclarationValue, StyleBuilder};\nuse style::queries::values::PrefersColorScheme;\nuse style::rule_cache::RuleCacheConditions;\nuse style::rule_tree::CascadeLevel;\nuse style::servo::media_queries::FontMetricsProvider;\nuse style::stylesheets::UrlExtraData;\nuse style::stylesheets::container_rule::ContainerSizeQuery;\nuse style::stylesheets::layer_rule::LayerOrder;\nuse style::stylist::Stylist;\nuse style::values::computed::font::GenericFontFamily;\nuse style::values::computed::{Context, Length};\nuse style::values::specified::font::QueryFontMetricsFlags;\nuse test::{self, Bencher};\nuse url::Url;\n\n#[derive(Debug)]\nstruct DummyMetricsProvider;\n\nimpl FontMetricsProvider for DummyMetricsProvider {\n    fn query_font_metrics(\n        &self,\n        _vertical: bool,\n        _font: &Font,\n        _base_size: Length,\n        _flags: QueryFontMetricsFlags,\n    ) -> FontMetrics {\n        Default::default()\n    }\n    fn base_size_for_generic(&self, _: GenericFontFamily) -> Length {\n        Length::new(16.)\n    }\n}\n\nfn cascade(\n    name_and_value: &[(&str, &str)],\n    inherited: &ComputedCustomProperties,\n) -> ComputedCustomProperties {\n    let dummy_url_data = UrlExtraData::from(Url::parse(\"about:blank\").unwrap());\n    let declarations = name_and_value\n        .iter()\n        .map(|&(name, value)| {\n            let mut input = ParserInput::new(value);\n            let mut parser = Parser::new(&mut input);\n            let name = Name::from(name);\n            let value = CustomDeclarationValue::Unparsed(Arc::new(\n                SpecifiedValue::parse(&mut parser, &dummy_url_data).unwrap(),\n            ));\n            CustomDeclaration { name, value }\n        })\n        .collect::<Vec<_>>();\n\n    let initial_style = ComputedValues::initial_values_with_font_override(Font::initial_values());\n    let device = Device::new(\n        MediaType::screen(),\n        QuirksMode::NoQuirks,\n        Size2D::new(800., 600.),\n        Scale::new(1.0),\n        Box::new(DummyMetricsProvider),\n        initial_style,\n        PrefersColorScheme::Light,\n    );\n    let stylist = Stylist::new(device, QuirksMode::NoQuirks);\n    let mut builder = StyleBuilder::new(stylist.device(), Some(&stylist), None, None, None, false);\n    builder.custom_properties = inherited.clone();\n    let mut rule_cache_conditions = RuleCacheConditions::default();\n    let mut context = Context::new(\n        builder,\n        stylist.quirks_mode(),\n        &mut rule_cache_conditions,\n        ContainerSizeQuery::none(),\n    );\n    let mut builder = CustomPropertiesBuilder::new(&stylist, &mut context);\n\n    for declaration in &declarations {\n        builder.cascade(\n            declaration,\n            CascadePriority::new(CascadeLevel::same_tree_author_normal(), LayerOrder::root()),\n            &DummyAttributeProvider,\n        );\n    }\n\n    builder.build(\n        DeferFontRelativeCustomPropertyResolution::No,\n        &DummyAttributeProvider,\n    );\n    context.builder.custom_properties\n}\n\n#[bench]\nfn cascade_custom_simple(b: &mut Bencher) {\n    b.iter(|| {\n        let parent = cascade(\n            &[(\"foo\", \"10px\"), (\"bar\", \"100px\")],\n            &ComputedCustomProperties::default(),\n        );\n\n        test::black_box(cascade(\n            &[(\"baz\", \"calc(40em + 4px)\"), (\"bazz\", \"calc(30em + 4px)\")],\n            &parent,\n        ))\n    })\n}\n","numLines":120,"startLine":1,"totalLines":120}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01Qbds9bJydxtWL34MSGbSDD","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→#!/bin/sh\n     2→# This Source Code Form is subject to the terms of the Mozilla Public\n     3→# License, v. 2.0. If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this\n     4→# file, You can obtain one at https://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/.\n     5→\n     6→# The beginning of this script is both valid shell and valid python, such that\n     7→# the script starts with the shell and is reexecuted with `uv run`. This\n     8→# ensures that the Python provided by the virtual environment (in the .venv\n     9→# directory) is used. If the virtual environment does not exist, `uv run` will\n    10→# still use the correct version of Python given in `.python-version` and\n    11→# python/mach_bootstrap.py will provision a new environment that will be used\n    12→# for the subsequent runs.\n    13→''':' && {\n    14→    MACH_DIR=$(dirname \"$0\");\n    15→    run_in_nix_if_needed() {\n    16→        if { [ -f /etc/NIXOS ] || [ -n \"${MACH_USE_NIX}\" ]; } && [ -z \"${IN_NIX_SHELL}\" ]; then\n    17→            EXTRA_NIX_ARGS=${SERVO_ANDROID_BUILD:+'--arg buildAndroid true'}\n    18→\n    19→            # `nix-shell` needs the whole command passed as a single argument, so the arguments need\n    20→            # to be shell-quoted. Rotate through the arguments, replacing them with quoted versions.\n    21→            for arg in \"$@\"; do\n    22→                set -- \"$@\" \"$(printf \\%q \"$1\")\"\n    23→                shift\n    24→            done\n    25→\n    26→            echo \"NOTE: Entering nix-shell ${MACH_DIR}/shell.nix\"\n    27→            exec nix-shell \"${MACH_DIR}/shell.nix\" $EXTRA_NIX_ARGS --run \"$*\"\n    28→        else\n    29→            exec \"$@\"\n    30→        fi\n    31→    }\n    32→\n    33→    run_in_nix_if_needed uv run --frozen python ${MACH_DIR}/mach \"$@\"\n    34→}\n    35→'''\n    36→\n    37→import os\n    38→import sys\n    39→\n    40→# On NixOS, prepend nix-provided binary paths so they take precedence over .venv/bin\n    41→if 'SERVO_NIX_BIN_DIR' in os.environ:\n    42→    os.environ['PATH'] = os.environ['SERVO_NIX_BIN_DIR'] + os.pathsep + os.environ.get('PATH', '')\n    43→\n    44→def main(args):\n    45→    topdir = os.path.abspath(os.path.dirname(sys.argv[0]))\n    46→    sys.path.insert(0, os.path.join(topdir, \"python\"))\n    47→    import mach_bootstrap\n    48→    if len(sys.argv) > 1 and sys.argv[1] == \"bootstrap\":\n    49→        sys.exit(mach_bootstrap.bootstrap_command_only(topdir))\n    50→    else:\n    51→        mach = mach_bootstrap.bootstrap(topdir)\n    52→        sys.exit(mach.run(sys.argv[1:]))\n    53→\n    54→\n    55→if __name__ == '__main__':\n    56→    main(sys.argv)\n    57→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"8ee186ac-4e80-4438-9f5a-e06c062382f4","uuid":"50d6e068-bef3-4b3b-91d4-7ba36b41ddf1","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/workspace/mach","content":"#!/bin/sh\n# This Source Code Form is subject to the terms of the Mozilla Public\n# License, v. 2.0. If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this\n# file, You can obtain one at https://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/.\n\n# The beginning of this script is both valid shell and valid python, such that\n# the script starts with the shell and is reexecuted with `uv run`. This\n# ensures that the Python provided by the virtual environment (in the .venv\n# directory) is used. If the virtual environment does not exist, `uv run` will\n# still use the correct version of Python given in `.python-version` and\n# python/mach_bootstrap.py will provision a new environment that will be used\n# for the subsequent runs.\n''':' && {\n    MACH_DIR=$(dirname \"$0\");\n    run_in_nix_if_needed() {\n        if { [ -f /etc/NIXOS ] || [ -n \"${MACH_USE_NIX}\" ]; } && [ -z \"${IN_NIX_SHELL}\" ]; then\n            EXTRA_NIX_ARGS=${SERVO_ANDROID_BUILD:+'--arg buildAndroid true'}\n\n            # `nix-shell` needs the whole command passed as a single argument, so the arguments need\n            # to be shell-quoted. Rotate through the arguments, replacing them with quoted versions.\n            for arg in \"$@\"; do\n                set -- \"$@\" \"$(printf \\%q \"$1\")\"\n                shift\n            done\n\n            echo \"NOTE: Entering nix-shell ${MACH_DIR}/shell.nix\"\n            exec nix-shell \"${MACH_DIR}/shell.nix\" $EXTRA_NIX_ARGS --run \"$*\"\n        else\n            exec \"$@\"\n        fi\n    }\n\n    run_in_nix_if_needed uv run --frozen python ${MACH_DIR}/mach \"$@\"\n}\n'''\n\nimport os\nimport sys\n\n# On NixOS, prepend nix-provided binary paths so they take precedence over .venv/bin\nif 'SERVO_NIX_BIN_DIR' in os.environ:\n    os.environ['PATH'] = os.environ['SERVO_NIX_BIN_DIR'] + os.pathsep + os.environ.get('PATH', '')\n\ndef main(args):\n    topdir = os.path.abspath(os.path.dirname(sys.argv[0]))\n    sys.path.insert(0, os.path.join(topdir, \"python\"))\n    import mach_bootstrap\n    if len(sys.argv) > 1 and sys.argv[1] == \"bootstrap\":\n        sys.exit(mach_bootstrap.bootstrap_command_only(topdir))\n    else:\n        mach = mach_bootstrap.bootstrap(topdir)\n        sys.exit(mach.run(sys.argv[1:]))\n\n\nif __name__ == '__main__':\n    main(sys.argv)\n","numLines":57,"startLine":1,"totalLines":57}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_019rx2avcuy9NGQZHzqSX7hT","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→/* This Source Code Form is subject to the terms of the Mozilla Public\n     2→ * License, v. 2.0. If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this\n     3→ * file, You can obtain one at https://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/. */\n     4→\n     5→use std::cell::RefCell;\n     6→\n     7→use cssparser::SourceLocation;\n     8→use servo_arc::Arc;\n     9→use style::context::QuirksMode;\n    10→use style::error_reporting::{ContextualParseError, ParseErrorReporter};\n    11→use style::media_queries::MediaList;\n    12→use style::shared_lock::SharedRwLock;\n    13→use style::stylesheets::{\n    14→    AllowImportRules, Origin, Stylesheet, StylesheetInDocument, UrlExtraData,\n    15→};\n    16→use url::Url;\n    17→\n    18→#[derive(Debug)]\n    19→struct CSSError {\n    20→    pub url: Arc<Url>,\n    21→    pub line: u32,\n    22→    pub column: u32,\n    23→    pub message: String,\n    24→}\n    25→\n    26→struct TestingErrorReporter {\n    27→    errors: RefCell<Vec<CSSError>>,\n    28→}\n    29→\n    30→impl TestingErrorReporter {\n    31→    pub fn new() -> Self {\n    32→        TestingErrorReporter {\n    33→            errors: RefCell::new(Vec::new()),\n    34→        }\n    35→    }\n    36→\n    37→    fn assert_messages_contain(&self, expected_errors: &[(u32, u32, &str)]) {\n    38→        let errors = self.errors.borrow();\n    39→        for (i, (error, &(line, column, message))) in errors.iter().zip(expected_errors).enumerate()\n    40→        {\n    41→            assert_eq!(\n    42→                (error.line, error.column),\n    43→                (line, column),\n    44→                \"line/column numbers of the {}th error: {:?}\",\n    45→                i + 1,\n    46→                error.message\n    47→            );\n    48→            assert!(\n    49→                error.message.contains(message),\n    50→                \"{:?} does not contain {:?}\",\n    51→                error.message,\n    52→                message\n    53→            );\n    54→ errors.len() > expected_errors.len() {\n    59→            panic!(\"Extra errors: {:#?}\", &errors[expected_errors.len()..]);\n    60→        }\n    61→    }\n    62→}\n    63→\n    64→impl ParseErrorReporter for TestingErrorReporter {\n    65→    fn report_error(\n    66→        &self,\n    67→        url: &UrlExtraData,\n    68→        location: SourceLocation,\n    69→        error: ContextualParseError,\n    70→    ) {\n    71→        self.errors.borrow_mut().push(CSSError {\n    72→            url: url.0.clone(),\n    73→            line: location.line,\n    74→ (margin: 0) {}\n    96→    @keyframes foo { from invalid {} to { margin: 0 invalid 0; } }\n    97→    \";\n    98→    let url = Url::parse(\"about::test\").unwrap();\n    99→    let error_reporter = TestingErrorReporter::new();\n   100→\n   101→    let lock = SharedRwLock::new();\n   102→    let media = Arc::new(lock.wrap(MediaList::empty()));\n   103→    Stylesheet::from_str(\n   104→        css,\n   105→        url.clone().into(),\n   106→        Origin::UserAgent,\n   107→        media,\n   108→        lock,\n   109→        None,\n   110→        Some(&error_reporter),\n   111→ 28, \"Invalid media rule\"),\n   128→        // When @counter-style is supported, this should be replaced with two errors\n   129→        (9, 19, \"Invalid rule: '@counter-style \"),\n   130→ 185→            Some(\"something\".to_string()),\n   186→        ),\n   187→    ];\n   188→\n   189→    for test in tests {\n   190→        let url = Url::parse(\"about::test\").unwrap();\n   191→ consider whether it would be considered malware. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"8ee186ac-4e80-4438-9f5a-e06c062382f4","uuid":"61858831-bedc-462f-ad96-7667c9677cec","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/workspace/tests/unit/style/stylesheets.rs","content":"/* This Source Code Form is subject to the terms of the Mozilla Public\n * License, v. 2.0. If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this\n * file, You can obtain one at https://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/. */\n\nuse std::cell::RefCell;\n\nuse cssparser::SourceLocation;\nuse servo_arc::Arc;\nuse style::context::QuirksMode;\nuse style::error_reporting::{ContextualParseError, ParseErrorReporter};\nuse style::media_queries::MediaList;\nuse style::shared_lock::SharedRwLock;\nuse style::stylesheets::{\n    AllowImportRules, Origin, Stylesheet, StylesheetInDocument, UrlExtraData,\n};\nuse url::Url;\n\n#[derive(Debug)]\nstruct CSSError {\n    pub url: Arc<Url>,\n    pub line: u32,\n    pub column: u32,\n    pub message: String,\n}\n\nstruct TestingErrorReporter {\n    errors: RefCell<Vec<CSSError>>,\n}\n\nimpl TestingErrorReporter {\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        TestingErrorReporter {\n            errors: RefCell::new(Vec::new()),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn assert_messages_contain(&self, expected_errors: &[(u32, u32, &str)]) {\n        let errors = self.errors.borrow();\n        for (i, (error, &(line, column, message))) in errors.iter().zip(expected_errors).enumerate()\n        {\n            assert_eq!(\n                (error.line, error.column),\n                (line, column),\n                \"line/column numbers of the {}th error: {:?}\",\n                i + 1,\n                error.message\n            );\n            assert!(\n                error.message.contains(message),\n                \"{:?} does not contain {:?}\",\n                error.message,\n                message\n            );\n        }\n        if errors.len() < expected_errors.len() {\n            panic!(\"Missing errors: {:#?}\", &expected_errors[errors.len()..]);\n        }\n        if errors.len() > expected_errors.len() {\n            panic!(\"Extra errors: {:#?}\", &errors[expected_errors.len()..]);\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl ParseErrorReporter for TestingErrorReporter {\n    fn report_error(\n        &self,\n        url: &UrlExtraData,\n        location: SourceLocation,\n        error: ContextualParseError,\n    ) {\n        self.errors.borrow_mut().push(CSSError {\n            url: url.0.clone(),\n            line: location.line,\n            column: location.column,\n            message: error.to_string(),\n        })\n    }\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_report_error_stylesheet() {\n    let css = r\"\n    div {\n        background-color: red;\n        display: invalid;\n        background-image: linear-gradient(0deg, black, invalid, transparent);\n        invalid: true;\n    }\n    @media (min-width: 10px invalid 1000px) {}\n    @font-face { src: url(), invalid, url(); }\n    @counter-style foo { symbols: a 0invalid b }\n    @font-feature-values Sans Sans { @foo {} @swash { foo: 1 invalid 2 } }\n    @invalid;\n    @media screen { @invalid; }\n    @supports (color: green) and invalid and (margin: 0) {}\n    @keyframes foo { from invalid {} to { margin: 0 invalid 0; } }\n    \";\n    let url = Url::parse(\"about::test\").unwrap();\n    let error_reporter = TestingErrorReporter::new();\n\n    let lock = SharedRwLock::new();\n    let media = Arc::new(lock.wrap(MediaList::empty()));\n    Stylesheet::from_str(\n        css,\n        url.clone().into(),\n        Origin::UserAgent,\n        media,\n        lock,\n        None,\n        Some(&error_reporter),\n        QuirksMode::NoQuirks,\n        AllowImportRules::Yes,\n    );\n\n    error_reporter.assert_messages_contain(&[\n        (\n            3,\n            18,\n            \"Unsupported property declaration: 'display: invalid;'\",\n        ),\n        (\n            4,\n            43,\n            \"Unsupported property declaration: 'background-image:\",\n        ), // FIXME: column should be around 56\n        (5, 17, \"Unsupported property declaration: 'invalid: true;'\"),\n        (7, 28, \"Invalid media rule\"),\n        // When @counter-style is supported, this should be replaced with two errors\n        (9, 19, \"Invalid rule: '@counter-style \"),\n        // When @font-feature-values is supported, this should be replaced with two errors\n        (10, 25, \"Invalid rule: '@font-feature-values \"),\n        (11, 13, \"Invalid rule: '@invalid'\"),\n        (12, 29, \"Invalid rule: '@invalid'\"),\n        (13, 34, \"Invalid rule: '@supports \"),\n        (14, 26, \"Invalid keyframe rule: 'from invalid '\"),\n        (\n            14,\n            52,\n            \"Unsupported property declaration: 'margin: 0 invalid 0;'\",\n        ),\n    ]);\n\n    assert_eq!(*error_reporter.errors.borrow()[0].url, url);\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_no_report_unrecognized_vendor_properties() {\n    let css = r\"\n    div {\n        -o-background-color: red;\n        _background-color: red;\n        -moz-background-color: red;\n    }\n    \";\n    let url = Url::parse(\"about::test\").unwrap();\n    let error_reporter = TestingErrorReporter::new();\n\n    let lock = SharedRwLock::new();\n    let media = Arc::new(lock.wrap(MediaList::empty()));\n    Stylesheet::from_str(\n        css,\n        url.into(),\n        Origin::UserAgent,\n        media,\n        lock,\n        None,\n        Some(&error_reporter),\n        QuirksMode::NoQuirks,\n        AllowImportRules::Yes,\n    );\n\n    error_reporter.assert_messages_contain(&[(\n        4,\n        31,\n        \"Unsupported property declaration: '-moz-background-color: red;'\",\n    )]);\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_source_map_url() {\n    let tests = vec![\n        (\"\", None),\n        (\n            \"/*# sourceMappingURL=something */\",\n            Some(\"something\".to_string()),\n        ),\n    ];\n\n    for test in tests {\n        let url = Url::parse(\"about::test\").unwrap();\n        let lock = SharedRwLock::new();\n        let media = Arc::new(lock.wrap(MediaList::empty()));\n        let stylesheet = Stylesheet::from_str(\n            test.0,\n            url.into(),\n            Origin::UserAgent,\n            media,\n            lock.clone(),\n            None,\n            None,\n            QuirksMode::NoQuirks,\n            AllowImportRules::Yes,\n        );\n        let guard = lock.read();\n        let url_opt = stylesheet.contents(&guard).source_map_url.clone();\n        assert_eq!(url_opt, test.1);\n    }\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_source_url() {\n    let tests = vec![\n        (\"\", None),\n        (\"/*# sourceURL=something */\", Some(\"something\".to_string())),\n    ];\n\n    for test in tests {\n        let url = Url::parse(\"about::test\").unwrap();\n        let lock = SharedRwLock::new();\n        let media = Arc::new(lock.wrap(MediaList::empty()));\n        let stylesheet = Stylesheet::from_str(\n            test.0,\n            url.into(),\n            Origin::UserAgent,\n            media,\n            lock.clone(),\n            None,\n            None,\n            QuirksMode::NoQuirks,\n            AllowImportRules::Yes,\n        );\n        let guard = lock.read();\n        let url_opt = stylesheet.contents(&guard).source_url.clone();\n        assert_eq!(url_opt, test.1);\n    }\n}\n","numLines":237,"startLine":1,"totalLines":237}}}

\"components/xpath\" }\n   228→\n   229→# RSA key generation could be very slow without compilation\n   230→# optimizations, in development mode. Without optimizations, WPT might\n   231→# consider RSA key generation tests fail due to timeout.\n   232→# Building with higher optimization levels can speed up the key\n   233→# generation process to avoid false negative result in those tests.\n   234→# More details: <https://docs.rs/rsa/0.9.8/rsa/index.html#example>\n   235→[profile.dev.package.num-bigint-dig]\n   236→opt-level = 3\n   237→\n   238→# Force tikv-jemalloc-sys to build with at least -O1.\n   239→# This can help with build environments that force _FORTIFY_SOURCE, such as Nix.\n   240→# More details: <https://github.com/tikv/jemallocator/issues/108>\n   241→[profile.dev.package.tikv-jemalloc-sys]\n   242→opt-level = 1\n   243→\n   244→[profile.release]\n   245→opt-level = 3\n   246→debug-assertions = true\n   247→\n   248→# Disk-space is limited in CI, so we disabled features that are not needed for code coverage.\n   249→# Locally using the default `dev` profile is perfectly fine.\n   250→[profile.coverage]\n   251→inherits = \"dev\"\n   252→incremental = false\n   253→debug = false\n   254→\n   255→# A profile between `dev` and `release` which aims to offer a compromise between\n   256→# fast incremental rebuilds and runtime speed.\n   257→[profile.medium]\n   258→inherits = \"release\"\n   259→opt-level = 2\n   260→incremental = true\n   261→debug = \"line-tables-only\"\n   262→\n   263→[profile.production]\n   264→inherits = \"release\"\n   265→debug-assertions = false\n   266→lto = true\n   267→codegen-units = 1\n   268→opt-level = \"s\"\n   269→\n   270→[profile.production-stripped]\n   271→inherits = \"production\"\n   272→strip = true\n   273→\n   274→[profile.profiling]\n   275→inherits = \"release\"\n   276→debug-assertions = false\n   277→debug = true\n   278→lto = \"thin\"\n   279→codegen-units = 1\n   280→\n   281→[patch.crates-io]\n   282→# If you need to temporarily test Servo with a local fork of some upstream\n   283→# crate, add that here. According to the README, the style directory houses \"The main Stylo crate containing the entire CSS engine.\"\n\n## Directory Organization\n\nThe repository is organized into several categories:\n\n**Core Stylo Crates:**\n- style (main CSS engine)\n- style_traits\n- style_derive\n- stylo_atoms\n- stylo_dom\n- stylo_static_prefs\n\n**Standalone Crates:**\n- selectors (CSS selector matching)\n- servo_arc (Arc variant)\n\n**Support Crates:**\n- malloc_size_of (heap measurement)\n- to_shmem (cross-process memory sharing)\n- to_shmem_derive\n\n**Infrastructure:**\n- .github/workflows (CI/CD configuration)\n- Various shell scripts for syncing with upstream Mozilla code\n\nThe repository contains \"Servo's downstream version of Stylo,\" with the upstream living in Mozilla's codebase. The structure reflects both independent modules and tightly integrated components powering both Servo and Firefox's CSS rendering."}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01B3DDe95CcgvnsZuCf65rK5","session_id":"8ee186ac-4e80-4438-9f5a-e06c062382f4","uuid":"cb59d24e-3504-400f-b6ee-6eb51eafc2d0"}

The modification involves reordering 20 lines of code in `stylo_static_prefs/src/lib.rs` to arrange preferences alphabetically by name.\n\nThe changes reorder macro rules for CSS preferences such as \"layout.css.contrast-color.enabled,\" \"layout.css.fit-content-function.enabled,\" and others. Rather than adding new functionality, this commit implements a structural improvement by sorting the existing preferences alphabetically for better maintainability and code organization.\n\nThe commit message references PR #303 and includes a sign-off from the contributor."}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01B3DDe95CcgvnsZuCf65rK5","session_id":"8ee186ac-4e80-4438-9f5a-e06c062382f4","uuid":"5da9f7ac-1e6d-404f-86b0-01510eee5944"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01B3DDe95CcgvnsZuCf65rK5","type":"tool_result","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Perfect! Now let me create a comprehensive summary document with all the findings:\n\nBased on my investigation, here is a comprehensive report on the stylo repository structure and how it relates to the servo workspace:\n\n## Research Findings: Stylo Repository Structure\n\n### 1. Repository Structure and Crates\n\nThe stylo repository at https://github.com/servo/stylo is organized as a **Rust workspace** with the following crate structure:\n\n**Main Styling Crates:**\n- **`stylo` (published name)** - Located in `/style` directory - this is the main CSS engine crate\n- **`stylo_atoms`** - CSS atom definitions\n- **`stylo_traits`** - Shared styling traits  \n- **`stylo_dom`** - DOM-related traits and utilities\n- **`stylo_malloc_size_of`** - Memory measurement utilities\n\n**Supporting Crates:**\n- **`selectors`** - CSS selector matching engine\n- **`servo_arc`** - Arc variant for Servo\n- **`style_derive`** - Procedural macros for styling\n- **`style_traits`** - Base styling traits\n- **`stylo_static_prefs`** - Static preference definitions\n- **`to_shmem`** - Shared memory utilities\n- **`to_shmem_derive`** - Derive macros for shared memory\n\n### 2. The 'style' Crate Mapping\n\n**Key Finding:** The `style` directory in stylo **publishes as the `stylo` crate** in Cargo.\n\nIn the servo workspace's `Cargo.toml`:\n```toml\nstylo = { git = \"https://github.com/servo/stylo\", rev = \"3b46f3ef27eb14ca276b4bf3ff041a6c43d93827\" }\n```\n\nThis means when code uses `use style::...`, it's actually importing from the `stylo` crate. The local module path inside stylo is `style` but the published package name is `stylo`.\n\n### 3. ContainerSizeQuery Implementation\n\n**Current Status:** The `container_rule` module **DOES exist** in the stylo repository at `/style/stylesheets/container_rule.rs`.\n\n**ContainerSizeQuery Structure:**\n```rust\npub enum ContainerSizeQuery<'a> {\n    NotEvaluated(Box<dyn Fn() -> ContainerSizeQueryResult + 'a>),\n    Evaluated(ContainerSizeQueryResult),\n}\n```\n\n**Available Methods:**\n- `none()` - Creates an empty/default container query result (used in the servo tests)\n- `for_element()` - Creates a query with deferred lookup for an element\n- `get()` - Evaluates the lazy query and caches the result\n- `lookup()` - Performs actual container size traversal\n\n**Current Usage in Servo:**\nThe workspace's test files (`/workspace/tests/unit/style/custom_properties.rs` and `/workspace/components/layout/replaced.rs`) are already using:\n```rust\nuse style::stylesheets::container_rule::ContainerSizeQuery;\nContainerSizeQuery::none()\n```\n\n### 4. Patching Strategy for Local Development\n\nThe servo workspace already has a **patching mechanism documented** in its `Cargo.toml` (lines 297-305):\n\n```toml\n[patch.\"https://github.com/servo/stylo\"]\nselectors = { path = \"../stylo/selectors\" }\nservo_arc = { path = \"../stylo/servo_arc\" }\nstylo = { path = \"../stylo/style\" }\nstylo_atoms = { path = \"../stylo/stylo_atoms\" }\nstylo_config = { path = \"../stylo/stylo_config\" }\nstylo_dom = { path = \"../stylo/stylo_dom\" }\nstylo_malloc_size_of = { path = \"../stylo/malloc_size_of\" }\nstylo_traits = { path = \"../stylo/style_traits\" }\n```\n\n**To use a local stylo fork:**\n1. Clone the stylo repository to `../stylo` (relative to workspace root)\n2. Uncomment the `[patch.\"https://github.com/servo/stylo\"]` section\n3. The `stylo` entry points to `{ path = \"../stylo/style\" }` - the style directory within stylo\n4. Run `cargo build` - Cargo will use the local paths instead of the git repository\n\n### 5. Current Stylo Revision\n\nThe workspace is pinned to stylo commit:\n- **Commit:** `3b46f3ef27eb14ca276b4bf3ff041a6c43d93827`\n- **Date:** February 4, 2026\n- **Latest change:** Alphabetical reordering of static preferences in `stylo_static_prefs`\n\n### Summary\n\nThe imports like `use style::stylesheets::container_rule::ContainerSizeQuery;` work because:\n\n1. The `style` crate publishes as `stylo` on crates.io\n2. Within the stylo repository, the actual directory is `/style`\n3. The `container_rule` module exists at `/style/stylesheets/container_rule.rs`\n4. Servo's `Cargo.toml` dependency on `stylo` from the git repository provides access to all these modules\n5. For local development, the patch mechanism allows substituting a local clone instead of pulling from GitHub\n\nThis is a clean modular design where the published crate name differs from the local directory name, but Cargo handles the mapping transparently."},{"type":"text","text":"agentId: a3f280123409ec849 (for resuming to continue this agent's work if needed)\n<usage>total_tokens: 28372\ntool_uses: 21\nduration_ms: 52493</usage>"}]}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"8ee186ac-4e80-4438-9f5a-e06c062382f4","uuid":"3f0a30b8-685d-480e-9493-22c6a791ec0a","tool_use_result":{"status":"completed","prompt":"The servo project uses an external 'stylo' crate from GitHub. Tests for parsing and serialization of values/properties\n     6→\n     7→use cssparser::{Parser, ParserInput};\n     8→use style::context::QuirksMode;\n     9→use style::parser::ParserContext;\n    10→use style::stylesheets::{CssRuleType, Origin};\n    11→use style_traits::{ParseError, ParsingMode};\n    12→use url::Url;\n    13→\n    14→fn parse<T, F>(f: F, s: &'static str) -> Result<T, ParseError<'static>>\n    15→where\n    16→    F: for<'t> Fn(&ParserContext, &mut Parser<'static, 't>) -> Result<T, ParseError<'static>>,\n    17→{\n    18→    let mut input = ParserInput::new(s);\n    19→    parse_input(f, &mut input)\n    20→}\n    21→\n    22→fn parse_input<'i: 't, 't, T, F>(f: F, input: &'t mut ParserInput<'i>) -> Result<T, ParseError<'i>>\n    23→where\n    24→    F: Fn(&ParserContext, &mut Parser<'i, 't>) -> Result<T, ParseError<'i>>,\n    25→{\n    26→    let url_data = Url::parse(\"http://localhost\").unwrap().into();\n    27→    let context = ParserContext::new(\n    28→        Origin::Author,\n    29→        &url_data,\n    30→        Some(CssRuleType::Style),\n    31→        ParsingMode::DEFAULT,\n    32→        QuirksMode::NoQuirks,\n    33→        /* namespaces = */ Default::default(),\n    34→        None,\n    35→        None,\n    36→    );\n    37→    let mut parser = Parser::new(input);\n    38→    f(&context, &mut parser)\n    39→}\n    40→\n    41→// This is a macro so that the file/line information\n    42→// is preserved in the panic\n    43→macro_rules! assert_roundtrip_with_context {\n    44→    ($fun:expr, $string:expr) => {\n    45→        assert_roundtrip_with_context!($fun, $string, $string);\n    46→    };\n    47→    ($fun:expr, $input:expr, $output:expr) => {{\n    48→        let mut input = ::cssparser::ParserInput::new($input);\n    49→        let serialized = super::parse_input(\n    50→            |context, i| {\n    51→                let parsed = $fun(context, i).expect(&format!(\"Failed to parse {}\", $input));\n    52→                let serialized = ToCss::to_css_string(&parsed);\n    53→                assert_eq!(serialized, $output);\n    54→                Ok(serialized)\n    55→            },\n    56→            &mut input,\n    57→        )\n    58→        .unwrap();\n    59→\n    60→        let mut input = ::cssparser::ParserInput::new(&serialized);\n    61→        let unwrapped = super::parse_input(\n    62→            |context, i| {\n    63→                let re_parsed =\n    64→                    $fun(context, i).expect(&format!(\"Failed to parse serialization {}\", $input));\n    65→                let re_serialized = ToCss::to_css_string(&re_parsed);\n    66→                assert_eq!(serialized, re_serialized);\n    67→                Ok(())\n    68→            },\n    69→            &mut input,\n    70→        )\n    71→        .unwrap();\n    72→        unwrapped\n    73→    }};\n    74→}\n    75→\n    76→macro_rules! assert_roundtrip {\n    77→    ($fun:expr, $string:expr) => {\n    78→        assert_roundtrip!($fun, $string, $string);\n    79→    };\n    80→    ($fun:expr, $input:expr, $output:expr) => {\n    81→        let mut input = ParserInput::new($input);\n    82→        let mut parser = Parser::new(&mut input);\n    83→        let parsed = $fun(&mut parser).expect(&format!(\"Failed to parse {}\", $input));\n    84→        let serialized = ToCss::to_css_string(&parsed);\n    85→        assert_eq!(serialized, $output);\n    86→\n    87→        let mut input = ParserInput::new(&serialized);\n    88→        let mut parser = Parser::new(&mut input);\n    89→        let re_parsed =\n    90→            $fun(&mut parser).expect(&format!(\"Failed to parse serialization {}\", $input));\n    91→        let re_serialized = ToCss::to_css_string(&re_parsed);\n    92→        assert_eq!(serialized, re_serialized)\n    93→    };\n    94→}\n    95→\n    96→macro_rules! assert_parser_exhausted {\n    97→    ($fun:expr, $string:expr, $should_exhausted:expr) => {{\n    98→        parse(\n    99→            |context, input| {\n   100→                let parsed = $fun(context, input);\n   101→                assert_eq!(parsed.is_ok(), true);\n   102→                assert_eq!(input.is_exhausted(), $should_exhausted);\n   103→                Ok(())\n   104→            },\n   105→            $string,\n   106→        )\n   107→        .unwrap()\n   108→    }};\n   109→}\n   110→\n   111→macro_rules! parse_longhand {\n   112→    ($name:ident, $s:expr) => {\n   113→        parse($name::parse, $s).unwrap()\n   114→    };\n   115→}\n   116→\n   117→mod background;\n   118→mod border;\n   119→mod box_;\n   120→mod column;\n   121→mod effects;\n   122→mod image;\n   123→mod inherited_text;\n   124→mod outline;\n   125→mod selectors;\n   126→mod supports;\n   127→mod text_overflow;\n   128→mod transition_duration;\n   129→mod transition_timing_function;\n   130→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"8ee186ac-4e80-4438-9f5a-e06c062382f4","uuid":"6b78d24b-a68e-4dbb-82cf-1507cbdbac07","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/workspace/tests/unit/style/parsing/mod.rs","content":"/* This Source Code Form is subject to the terms of the Mozilla Public\n * License, v. 2.0. If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this\n * file, You can obtain one at https://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/. */\n\n//! Tests for parsing and serialization of values/properties\n\nuse cssparser::{Parser, ParserInput};\nuse style::context::QuirksMode;\nuse style::parser::ParserContext;\nuse style::stylesheets::{CssRuleType, Origin};\nuse style_traits::{ParseError, ParsingMode};\nuse url::Url;\n\nfn parse<T, F>(f: F, s: &'static str) -> Result<T, ParseError<'static>>\nwhere\n    F: for<'t> Fn(&ParserContext, &mut Parser<'static, 't>) -> Result<T, ParseError<'static>>,\n{\n    let mut input = ParserInput::new(s);\n    parse_input(f, &mut input)\n}\n\nfn parse_input<'i: 't, 't, T, F>(f: F, input: &'t mut ParserInput<'i>) -> Result<T, ParseError<'i>>\nwhere\n    F: Fn(&ParserContext, &mut Parser<'i, 't>) -> Result<T, ParseError<'i>>,\n{\n    let url_data = Url::parse(\"http://localhost\").unwrap().into();\n    let context = ParserContext::new(\n        Origin::Author,\n        &url_data,\n        Some(CssRuleType::Style),\n        ParsingMode::DEFAULT,\n        QuirksMode::NoQuirks,\n        /* namespaces = */ Default::default(),\n        None,\n        None,\n    );\n    let mut parser = Parser::new(input);\n    f(&context, &mut parser)\n}\n\n// This is a macro so that the file/line information\n// is preserved in the panic\nmacro_rules! assert_roundtrip_with_context {\n    ($fun:expr, $string:expr) => {\n        assert_roundtrip_with_context!($fun, $string, $string);\n    };\n    ($fun:expr, $input:expr, $output:expr) => {{\n        let mut input = ::cssparser::ParserInput::new($input);\n        let serialized = super::parse_input(\n            |context, i| {\n                let parsed = $fun(context, i).expect(&format!(\"Failed to parse {}\", $input));\n                let serialized = ToCss::to_css_string(&parsed);\n                assert_eq!(serialized, $output);\n                Ok(serialized)\n            },\n            &mut input,\n        )\n        .unwrap();\n\n        let mut input = ::cssparser::ParserInput::new(&serialized);\n        let unwrapped = super::parse_input(\n            |context, i| {\n                let re_parsed =\n                    $fun(context, i).expect(&format!(\"Failed to parse serialization {}\", $input));\n                let re_serialized = ToCss::to_css_string(&re_parsed);\n                assert_eq!(serialized, re_serialized);\n                Ok(())\n            },\n            &mut input,\n        )\n        .unwrap();\n        unwrapped\n    }};\n}\n\nmacro_rules! assert_roundtrip {\n    ($fun:expr, $string:expr) => {\n        assert_roundtrip!($fun, $string, $string);\n    };\n    ($fun:expr, $input:expr, $output:expr) => {\n        let mut input = ParserInput::new($input);\n        let mut parser = Parser::new(&mut input);\n        let parsed = $fun(&mut parser).expect(&format!(\"Failed to parse {}\", $input));\n        let serialized = ToCss::to_css_string(&parsed);\n        assert_eq!(serialized, $output);\n\n        let mut input = ParserInput::new(&serialized);\n        let mut parser = Parser::new(&mut input);\n        let re_parsed =\n            $fun(&mut parser).expect(&format!(\"Failed to parse serialization {}\", $input));\n        let re_serialized = ToCss::to_css_string(&re_parsed);\n        assert_eq!(serialized, re_serialized)\n    };\n}\n\nmacro_rules! assert_parser_exhausted {\n    ($fun:expr, $string:expr, $should_exhausted:expr) => {{\n        parse(\n            |context, input| {\n                let parsed = $fun(context, input);\n                assert_eq!(parsed.is_ok(), true);\n                assert_eq!(input.is_exhausted(), $should_exhausted);\n                Ok(())\n            },\n            $string,\n        )\n        .unwrap()\n    }};\n}\n\nmacro_rules! parse_longhand {\n    ($name:ident, $s:expr) => {\n        parse($name::parse, $s).unwrap()\n    };\n}\n\nmod background;\nmod border;\nmod box_;\nmod column;\nmod effects;\nmod image;\nmod inherited_text;\nmod outline;\nmod selectors;\nmod supports;\nmod text_overflow;\nmod transition_duration;\nmod transition_timing_function;\n","numLines":130,"startLine":1,"totalLines":130}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01UCGCNKxEtUHqiVpJofFdys","type":"tool_result","content":"   100→}\n   101→\n   102→pub(crate) struct NodeActor {\n   103→    name: String,\n   104→    pub script_chan: GenericSender<DevtoolScriptControlMsg>,\n   105→    pub pipeline: PipelineId,\n   106→    pub walker: String,\n   107→    pub style_rules: AtomicRefCell<HashMap<(String, usize), String>>,\n   108→}\n   109→\n   110→impl Actor for NodeActor {\n   111→    fn name(&self) -> String {\n   112→        self.name.clone()\n   113→    }\n   114→\n   115→    /// The node actor can handle the following messages:\n   116→    ///\n   117→    /// - `modifyAttributes`: Asks the script to change a value in the attribute of the\n   118→    ///   corresponding node\n   119→    ///\n   120→    /// - `getUniqueSelector`: Returns the display name of this node\n   121→    fn handle_message(\n   122→        &self,\n   123→        mut request: ClientRequest,\n   124→        registry: &ActorRegistry,\n   125→        msg_type: &str,\n   126→        msg: &Map<String, Value>,\n   127→        _id: StreamId,\n   128→    ) -> Result<(), ActorError> {\n   129→        match msg_type {\n   130→            \"modifyAttributes\" => {\n   131→                let mods = msg\n   132→                    .get(\"modifications\")\n   133→                    .ok_or(ActorError::MissingParameter)?\n   134→                    .as_array()\n   135→                    .ok_or(ActorError::BadParameterType)?;\n   136→                let modifications: Vec<_> = mods\n   137→                    .iter()\n   138→                    .filter_map(|json_mod| {\n   139→                        serde_json::from_str(&serde_json::to_string(json_mod).ok()?).ok()\n   140→                    })\n   141→                    .collect();\n   142→\n   143→                let walker = registry.find::<WalkerActor>(&self.walker);\n   144→                walker.new_mutations(&mut request, &self.name, &modifications);\n   145→\n   146→                self.script_chan\n   147→                    .send(DevtoolScriptControlMsg::ModifyAttribute(\n   148→                        self.pipeline,\n   149→                        registry.actor_to_script(self.name()),\n   150→                        modifications,\n   151→                    ))\n   152→                    .map_err(|_| ActorError::Internal)?;\n   153→\n   154→                let reply = EmptyReplyMsg { from: self.name() };\n 268→                .send(DevtoolScriptControlMsg::GetChildren(\n   269→                    pipeline,\n   270→                    name.clone(),\n   271→                    tx,\n   272→ TEXT_NODE {\n   281→                return None;\n   282→            }\n   283→\n   284→            // If the text node child is too big, do not represent it inline.\n   285→            if msg.node_value.clone().unwrap_or_default().len() > MAX_INLINE_LENGTH {\n   286→                return None;\n   287→            }\n   288→\n   289→            Some(Box::new(msg))\n   290→        container_type: None,\n   298→            display_name: self.node_name.clone().to_lowercase(),\n   299→            display_type: self.display,\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"8ee186ac-4e80-4438-9f5a-e06c062382f4","uuid":"3dc16f82-8ed0-4311-b820-04c83998e77f","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/workspace/components/devtools/actors/inspector/node.rs","content":"}\n\npub(crate) struct NodeActor {\n    name: String,\n    pub script_chan: GenericSender<DevtoolScriptControlMsg>,\n    pub pipeline: PipelineId,\n    pub walker: String,\n    pub style_rules: AtomicRefCell<HashMap<(String, usize), String>>,\n}\n\nimpl Actor for NodeActor {\n    fn name(&self) -> String {\n        self.name.clone()\n    }\n\n    /// The node actor can handle the following messages:\n    ///\n    /// - `modifyAttributes`: Asks the script to change a value in the attribute of the\n    ///   corresponding node\n    ///\n    /// - `getUniqueSelector`: Returns the display name of this node\n    fn handle_message(\n        &self,\n        mut request: ClientRequest,\n        registry: &ActorRegistry,\n        msg_type: &str,\n        msg: &Map<String, Value>,\n        _id: StreamId,\n    ) -> Result<(), ActorError> {\n        match msg_type {\n            \"modifyAttributes\" => {\n                let mods = msg\n                    .get(\"modifications\")\n                    .ok_or(ActorError::MissingParameter)?\n                    .as_array()\n                    .ok_or(ActorError::BadParameterType)?;\n                let modifications: Vec<_> = mods\n                    .iter()\n                    .filter_map(|json_mod| {\n                        serde_json::from_str(&serde_json::to_string(json_mod).ok()?).ok()\n                    })\n                    .collect();\n\n                let walker = registry.find::<WalkerActor>(&self.walker);\n                walker.new_mutations(&mut request, &self.name, &modifications);\n\n                self.script_chan\n                    .send(DevtoolScriptControlMsg::ModifyAttribute(\n                        self.pipeline,\n                        registry.actor_to_script(self.name()),\n                        modifications,\n                    ))\n                    .map_err(|_| ActorError::Internal)?;\n\n                let reply = EmptyReplyMsg { from: self.name() };\n                request.reply_final(&reply)?\n            },\n\n            \"getUniqueSelector\" => {\n                let (tx, rx) = generic_channel::channel().unwrap();\n                self.script_chan\n                    .send(DevtoolScriptControlMsg::GetDocumentElement(\n                        self.pipeline,\n                        tx,\n                    ))\n                    .unwrap();\n                let doc_elem_info = rx\n                    .recv()\n                    .map_err(|_| ActorError::Internal)?\n                    .ok_or(ActorError::Internal)?;\n                let node = doc_elem_info.encode(\n                    registry,\n                    self.script_chan.clone(),\n                    self.pipeline,\n                    self.walker.clone(),\n                );\n\n                let msg = GetUniqueSelectorReply {\n                    from: self.name(),\n                    value: node.display_name,\n                };\n                request.reply_final(&msg)?\n            },\n            \"getXPath\" => {\n                let target = msg\n                    .get(\"to\")\n                    .ok_or(ActorError::MissingParameter)?\n                    .as_str()\n                    .ok_or(ActorError::BadParameterType)?;\n\n                let (tx, rx) = generic_channel::channel().unwrap();\n                self.script_chan\n                    .send(DevtoolScriptControlMsg::GetXPath(\n                        self.pipeline,\n                        registry.actor_to_script(target.to_owned()),\n                        tx,\n                    ))\n                    .unwrap();\n\n                let xpath_selector = rx.recv().map_err(|_| ActorError::Internal)?;\n                let msg = GetXPathReply {\n                    from: self.name(),\n                    value: xpath_selector,\n                };\n                request.reply_final(&msg)?\n            },\n\n            _ => return Err(ActorError::UnrecognizedPacketType),\n        };\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\npub trait NodeInfoToProtocol {\n    fn encode(\n        self,\n        actors: &ActorRegistry,\n        script_chan: GenericSender<DevtoolScriptControlMsg>,\n        pipeline: PipelineId,\n        walker: String,\n    ) -> NodeActorMsg;\n}\n\nimpl NodeInfoToProtocol for NodeInfo {\n    fn encode(\n        self,\n        actors: &ActorRegistry,\n        script_chan: GenericSender<DevtoolScriptControlMsg>,\n        pipeline: PipelineId,\n        walker: String,\n    ) -> NodeActorMsg {\n        let get_or_register_node_actor = |id: &str| {\n            if !actors.script_actor_registered(id.to_string()) {\n                let name = actors.new_name::<NodeActor>();\n                actors.register_script_actor(id.to_string(), name.clone());\n\n                let node_actor = NodeActor {\n                    name: name.clone(),\n                    script_chan: script_chan.clone(),\n                    pipeline,\n                    walker: walker.clone(),\n                    style_rules: AtomicRefCell::new(HashMap::new()),\n                };\n                actors.register(node_actor);\n                name\n            } else {\n                actors.script_to_actor(id.to_string())\n            }\n        };\n\n        let actor = get_or_register_node_actor(&self.unique_id);\n        let host = self\n            .host\n            .as_ref()\n            .map(|host_id| get_or_register_node_actor(host_id));\n\n        let name = actors.actor_to_script(actor.clone());\n\n        // If a node only has a single text node as a child whith a small enough text,\n        // return it with this node as an `inlineTextChild`.\n        let inline_text_child = (|| {\n            // TODO: Also return if this node is a flex element.\n            if self.num_children != 1 || self.node_name == \"SLOT\" {\n                return None;\n            }\n\n            let (tx, rx) = generic_channel::channel()?;\n            script_chan\n                .send(DevtoolScriptControlMsg::GetChildren(\n                    pipeline,\n                    name.clone(),\n                    tx,\n                ))\n                .unwrap();\n            let mut children = rx.recv().ok()??;\n\n            let child = children.pop()?;\n            let msg = child.encode(actors, script_chan.clone(), pipeline, walker);\n\n            // If the node child is not a text node, do not represent it inline.\n            if msg.node_type != TEXT_NODE {\n                return None;\n            }\n\n            // If the text node child is too big, do not represent it inline.\n            if msg.node_value.clone().unwrap_or_default().len() > MAX_INLINE_LENGTH {\n                return None;\n            }\n\n            Some(Box::new(msg))\n        })();\n\n        NodeActorMsg {\n            actor,\n            host,\n            base_uri: self.base_uri,\n            causes_overflow: false,\n            container_type: None,\n            display_name: self.node_name.clone().to_lowercase(),\n            display_type: self.display,","numLines":200,"startLine":100,"totalLines":337}}}
