{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01X9XwnRwtjEF3LWCyJWjJjh","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→/* This Source Code Form is subject to the terms of the Mozilla Public\n     2→ * License, v. 2.0. If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this\n     3→ * file, You can obtain one at https://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/. */\n     4→\n     5→//! A [`@container`][container] rule.\n     6→//!\n     7→//! [container]: https://drafts.csswg.org/css-contain-3/#container-rule\n     8→\n     9→use crate::computed_value_flags::ComputedValueFlags;\n    10→use crate::derives::*;\n    11→use crate::dom::TElement;\n    12→use crate::logical_geometry::{LogicalSize, WritingMode};\n    13→use crate::parser::ParserContext;\n    14→use crate::properties::ComputedValues;\n    15→use crate::queries::feature::{AllowsRanges, Evaluator, FeatureFlags, QueryFeatureDescription};\n    16→use crate::queries::values::Orientation;\n    17→use crate::queries::{FeatureType, QueryCondition};\n    18→use crate::shared_lock::{\n    19→    DeepCloneWithLock, Locked, SharedRwLock, SharedRwLockReadGuard, ToCssWithGuard,\n    20→};\n    21→use crate::stylesheets::{CssRules, CustomMediaEvaluator};\n    22→use crate::stylist::Stylist;\n    23→use crate::values::computed::{CSSPixelLength, ContainerType, Context, Ratio};\n    24→use crate::values::specified::ContainerName;\n    25→use app_units::Au;\n    26→use cssparser::{Parser, SourceLocation};\n    27→use euclid::default::Size2D;\n    28→#[cfg(feature = \"gecko\")]\n    29→use malloc_size_of::{MallocSizeOfOps, MallocUnconditionalShallowSizeOf};\n    30→use selectors::kleene_value::KleeneValue;\n    31→use servo_arc::Arc;\n    32→use std::fmt::{self, Write};\n    33→use style_traits::{CssStringWriter, CssWriter, ParseError, ToCss};\n    34→\n    35→/// A container rule.\n    36→#[derive(Debug, ToShmem)]\n    37→pub struct ContainerRule {\n    38→    /// The container query and name.\n    39→    pub condition: Arc<ContainerCondition>,\n    40→    /// The nested rules inside the block.\n    41→    pub rules: Arc<Locked<CssRules>>,\n    42→    /// The source position where this rule was found.\n    43→    pub source_location: SourceLocation,\n    44→}\n    45→\n    46→impl ContainerRule {\n    47→    /// Returns the query condition.\n    48→    pub fn query_condition(&self) -> &QueryCondition {\n    49→        &self.condition.condition\n    50→    }\n    51→\n    52→    /// Returns the query name filter.\n    53→    pub fn container_name(&self) -> &ContainerName {\n    54→        &self.condition.name\n    55→    }\n    56→\n    57→    /// Measure heap usage.\n    58→    #[cfg(feature = \"gecko\")]\n    59→    pub fn size_of(&self, guard: &SharedRwLockReadGuard, ops: &mut MallocSizeOfOps) -> usize {\n    60→        // Measurement of other fields may be added later.\n    61→        self.rules.unconditional_shallow_size_of(ops)\n    62→            + self.rules.read_with(guard).size_of(guard, ops)\n    63→    }\n    64→}\n    65→\n    66→impl DeepCloneWithLock for ContainerRule {\n    67→    fn deep_clone_with_lock(&self, lock: &SharedRwLock, guard: &SharedRwLockReadGuard) -> Self {\n    68→        let rules = self.rules.read_with(guard);\n    69→        Self {\n    70→            condition: self.condition.clone(),\n    71→            rules: Arc::new(lock.wrap(rules.deep_clone_with_lock(lock, guard))),\n    72→            source_location: self.source_location.clone(),\n    73→        }\n    74→    }\n    75→}\n    76→\n    77→impl ToCssWithGuard for ContainerRule {\n    78→    fn to_css(&self, guard: &SharedRwLockReadGuard, dest: &mut CssStringWriter) -> fmt::Result {\n    79→        dest.write_str(\"@container \")?;\n    80→        {\n    81→            let mut writer = CssWriter::new(dest);\n    82→            if !self.condition.name.is_none() {\n    83→                self.condition.name.to_css(&mut writer)?;\n    84→                writer.write_char(' ')?;\n    85→            }\n    86→            self.condition.condition.to_css(&mut writer)?;\n    87→        }\n    88→        self.rules.read_with(guard).to_css_block(guard, dest)\n    89→    }\n    90→}\n    91→\n    92→/// A container condition and filter, combined.\n    93→#[derive(Debug, ToShmem, ToCss)]\n    94→pub struct ContainerCondition {\n    95→    #[css(skip_if = \"ContainerName::is_none\")]\n    96→    name: ContainerName,\n    97→    condition: QueryCondition,\n    98→    #[css(skip)]\n    99→    flags: FeatureFlags,\n   100→}\n   101→\n   102→/// The result of a successful container query lookup.\n   103→pub struct ContainerLookupResult<E> {\n   104→    /// The relevant container.\n   105→    pub element: E,\n   106→    /// The sizing / writing-mode information of the container.\n   107→    pub info: ContainerInfo,\n   108→    /// The style of the element.\n   109→    pub style: Arc<ComputedValues>,\n   110→}\n   111→\n   112→fn container_type_axes(ty_: ContainerType, wm: WritingMode) -> FeatureFlags {\n   113→    if ty_.intersects(ContainerType::SIZE) {\n   114→        FeatureFlags::all_container_axes()\n   115→    } else if ty_.intersects(ContainerType::INLINE_SIZE) {\n   116→        let physical_axis = if wm.is_vertical() {\n   117→            FeatureFlags::CONTAINER_REQUIRES_HEIGHT_AXIS\n   118→        } else {\n   119→            FeatureFlags::CONTAINER_REQUIRES_WIDTH_AXIS\n   120→        };\n   121→        FeatureFlags::CONTAINER_REQUIRES_INLINE_AXIS | physical_axis\n   122→    } else {\n   123→        FeatureFlags::empty()\n   124→    }\n   125→}\n   126→\n   127→enum TraversalResult<T> {\n   128→    InProgress,\n   129→    StopTraversal,\n   130→    Done(T),\n   131→}\n   132→\n   133→fn traverse_container<E, F, R>(\n   134→    mut e: E,\n   135→    originating_element_style: Option<&ComputedValues>,\n   136→    evaluator: F,\n   137→) -> Option<(E, R)>\n   138→where\n   139→    E: TElement,\n   140→    F: Fn(E, Option<&ComputedValues>) -> TraversalResult<R>,\n   141→{\n   142→    if originating_element_style.is_some() {\n   143→        match evaluator(e, originating_element_style) {\n   144→            TraversalResult::InProgress => {},\n   145→            TraversalResult::StopTraversal => return None,\n   146→            TraversalResult::Done(result) => return Some((e, result)),\n   147→        }\n   148→    }\n   149→    while let Some(element) = e.traversal_parent() {\n   150→        match evaluator(element, None) {\n   151→            TraversalResult::InProgress => {},\n   152→            TraversalResult::StopTraversal => return None,\n   153→            TraversalResult::Done(result) => return Some((element, result)),\n   154→        }\n   155→        e = element;\n   156→    }\n   157→\n   158→    None\n   159→}\n   160→\n   161→impl ContainerCondition {\n   162→    /// Parse a container condition.\n   163→    pub fn parse<'a>(\n   164→        context: &ParserContext,\n   165→        input: &mut Parser<'a, '_>,\n   166→    ) -> Result<Self, ParseError<'a>> {\n   167→        let name = input\n   168→            .try_parse(|input| ContainerName::parse_for_query(context, input))\n   169→            .ok()\n   170→            .unwrap_or_else(ContainerName::none);\n   171→        let condition = QueryCondition::parse(context, input, FeatureType::Container)?;\n   172→        let flags = condition.cumulative_flags();\n   173→        Ok(Self {\n   174→            name,\n   175→            condition,\n   176→            flags,\n   177→        })\n   178→    }\n   179→\n   180→    fn valid_container_info<E>(\n   181→        &self,\n   182→        potential_container: E,\n   183→        originating_element_style: Option<&ComputedValues>,\n   184→    ) -> TraversalResult<ContainerLookupResult<E>>\n   185→    where\n   186→        E: TElement,\n   187→    {\n   188→        let data;\n   189→        let style = match originating_element_style {\n   190→            Some(s) => s,\n   191→            None => {\n   192→                data = match potential_container.borrow_data() {\n   193→                    Some(d) => d,\n   194→                    None => return TraversalResult::InProgress,\n   195→                };\n   196→                &**data.styles.primary()\n   197→            },\n   198→        };\n   199→        let wm = style.writing_mode;\n   200→        let box_style = style.get_box();\n   201→\n   202→        // Filter by container-type.\n   203→        let container_type = box_style.clone_container_type();\n   204→        let available_axes = container_type_axes(container_type, wm);\n   205→        if !available_axes.contains(self.flags.container_axes()) {\n   206→            return TraversalResult::InProgress;\n   207→        }\n   208→\n   209→        // Filter by container-name.\n   210→        let container_name = box_style.clone_container_name();\n   211→        for filter_name in self.name.0.iter() {\n   212→            if !container_name.0.contains(filter_name) {\n   213→                return TraversalResult::InProgress;\n   214→            }\n   215→        }\n   216→\n   217→        let size = potential_container.query_container_size(&box_style.clone_display());\n   218→        let style = style.to_arc();\n   219→        TraversalResult::Done(ContainerLookupResult {\n   220→            element: potential_container,\n   221→            info: ContainerInfo { size, wm },\n   222→            style,\n   223→        })\n   224→    }\n   225→\n   226→    /// Performs container lookup for a given element.\n   227→    pub fn find_container<E>(\n   228→        &self,\n   229→        e: E,\n   230→        originating_element_style: Option<&ComputedValues>,\n   231→    ) -> Option<ContainerLookupResult<E>>\n   232→    where\n   233→        E: TElement,\n   234→    {\n   235→        match traverse_container(\n   236→            e,\n   237→            originating_element_style,\n   238→            |element, originating_element_style| {\n   239→                self.valid_container_info(element, originating_element_style)\n   240→            },\n   241→        ) {\n   242→            Some((_, result)) => Some(result),\n   243→            None => None,\n   244→        }\n   245→    }\n   246→\n   247→    /// Tries to match a container query condition for a given element.\n   248→    pub(crate) fn matches<E>(\n   249→        &self,\n   250→        stylist: &Stylist,\n   251→        element: E,\n   252→        originating_element_style: Option<&ComputedValues>,\n   253→        invalidation_flags: &mut ComputedValueFlags,\n   254→    ) -> KleeneValue\n   255→    where\n   256→        E: TElement,\n   257→    {\n   258→        let result = self.find_container(element, originating_element_style);\n   259→        let (container, info) = match result {\n   260→            Some(r) => (Some(r.element), Some((r.info, r.style))),\n   261→            None => (None, None),\n   262→        };\n   263→        // Set up the lookup for the container in question, as the condition may be using container\n   264→        // query lengths.\n   265→        let size_query_container_lookup = ContainerSizeQuery::for_option_element(\n   266→            container, /* known_parent_style = */ None, /* is_pseudo = */ false,\n   267→        );\n   268→        Context::for_container_query_evaluation(\n   269→            stylist.device(),\n   270→            Some(stylist),\n   271→            info,\n   272→            size_query_container_lookup,\n   273→            |context| {\n   274→                let matches = self\n   275→                    .condition\n   276→                    .matches(context, &mut CustomMediaEvaluator::none());\n   277→                if context\n   278→                    .style()\n   279→                    .flags()\n   280→                    .contains(ComputedValueFlags::USES_VIEWPORT_UNITS)\n   281→                {\n   282→                    // TODO(emilio): Might need something similar to improve\n   283→                    // invalidation of font relative container-query lengths.\n   284→                    invalidation_flags\n   285→                        .insert(ComputedValueFlags::USES_VIEWPORT_UNITS_ON_CONTAINER_QUERIES);\n   286→                }\n   287→                matches\n   288→            },\n   289→        )\n   290→    }\n   291→}\n   292→\n   293→/// Information needed to evaluate an individual container query.\n   294→#[derive(Copy, Clone)]\n   295→pub struct ContainerInfo {\n   296→    size: Size2D<Option<Au>>,\n   297→    wm: WritingMode,\n   298→}\n   299→\n   300→impl ContainerInfo {\n   301→    fn size(&self) -> Option<Size2D<Au>> {\n   302→        Some(Size2D::new(self.size.width?, self.size.height?))\n   303→    }\n   304→}\n   305→\n   306→fn eval_width(context: &Context) -> Option<CSSPixelLength> {\n   307→    let info = context.container_info.as_ref()?;\n   308→    Some(CSSPixelLength::new(info.size.width?.to_f32_px()))\n   309→}\n   310→\n   311→fn eval_height(context: &Context) -> Option<CSSPixelLength> {\n   312→    let info = context.container_info.as_ref()?;\n   313→    Some(CSSPixelLength::new(info.size.height?.to_f32_px()))\n   314→}\n   315→\n   316→fn eval_inline_size(context: &Context) -> Option<CSSPixelLength> {\n   317→    let info = context.container_info.as_ref()?;\n   318→    Some(CSSPixelLength::new(\n   319→        LogicalSize::from_physical(info.wm, info.size)\n   320→            .inline?\n   321→            .to_f32_px(),\n   322→    ))\n   323→}\n   324→\n   325→fn eval_block_size(context: &Context) -> Option<CSSPixelLength> {\n   326→    let info = context.container_info.as_ref()?;\n   327→    Some(CSSPixelLength::new(\n   328→        LogicalSize::from_physical(info.wm, info.size)\n   329→            .block?\n   330→            .to_f32_px(),\n   331→    ))\n   332→}\n   333→\n   334→fn eval_aspect_ratio(context: &Context) -> Option<Ratio> {\n   335→    let info = context.container_info.as_ref()?;\n   336→    Some(Ratio::new(\n   337→        info.size.width?.0 as f32,\n   338→        info.size.height?.0 as f32,\n   339→    ))\n   340→}\n   341→\n   342→fn eval_orientation(context: &Context, value: Option<Orientation>) -> KleeneValue {\n   343→    let size = match context.container_info.as_ref().and_then(|info| info.size()) {\n   344→        Some(size) => size,\n   345→        None => return KleeneValue::Unknown,\n   346→    };\n   347→    KleeneValue::from(Orientation::eval(size, value))\n   348→}\n   349→\n   350→/// https://drafts.csswg.org/css-contain-3/#container-features\n   351→///\n   352→/// TODO: Support style queries, perhaps.\n   353→pub static CONTAINER_FEATURES: [QueryFeatureDescription; 6] = [\n   354→    feature!(\n   355→        atom!(\"width\"),\n   356→        AllowsRanges::Yes,\n   357→        Evaluator::OptionalLength(eval_width),\n   358→        FeatureFlags::CONTAINER_REQUIRES_WIDTH_AXIS,\n   359→    ),\n   360→    feature!(\n   361→        atom!(\"height\"),\n   362→        AllowsRanges::Yes,\n   363→        Evaluator::OptionalLength(eval_height),\n   364→        FeatureFlags::CONTAINER_REQUIRES_HEIGHT_AXIS,\n   365→    ),\n   366→    feature!(\n   367→        atom!(\"inline-size\"),\n   368→        AllowsRanges::Yes,\n   369→        Evaluator::OptionalLength(eval_inline_size),\n   370→        FeatureFlags::CONTAINER_REQUIRES_INLINE_AXIS,\n   371→    ),\n   372→    feature!(\n   373→        atom!(\"block-size\"),\n   374→        AllowsRanges::Yes,\n   375→        Evaluator::OptionalLength(eval_block_size),\n   376→        FeatureFlags::CONTAINER_REQUIRES_BLOCK_AXIS,\n   377→    ),\n   378→    feature!(\n   379→        atom!(\"aspect-ratio\"),\n   380→        AllowsRanges::Yes,\n   381→        Evaluator::OptionalNumberRatio(eval_aspect_ratio),\n   382→        // XXX from_bits_truncate is const, but the pipe operator isn't, so this\n   383→        // works around it.\n   384→        FeatureFlags::from_bits_truncate(\n   385→            FeatureFlags::CONTAINER_REQUIRES_BLOCK_AXIS.bits()\n   386→                | FeatureFlags::CONTAINER_REQUIRES_INLINE_AXIS.bits()\n   387→        ),\n   388→    ),\n   389→    feature!(\n   390→        atom!(\"orientation\"),\n   391→        AllowsRanges::No,\n   392→        keyword_evaluator!(eval_orientation, Orientation),\n   393→        FeatureFlags::from_bits_truncate(\n   394→            FeatureFlags::CONTAINER_REQUIRES_BLOCK_AXIS.bits()\n   395→                | FeatureFlags::CONTAINER_REQUIRES_INLINE_AXIS.bits()\n   396→        ),\n   397→    ),\n   398→];\n   399→\n   400→/// Result of a container size query, signifying the hypothetical containment boundary in terms of physical axes.\n   401→/// Defined by up to two size containers. Queries on logical axes are resolved with respect to the querying\n   402→/// element's writing mode.\n   403→#[derive(Copy, Clone, Default)]\n   404→pub struct ContainerSizeQueryResult {\n   405→    width: Option<Au>,\n   406→    height: Option<Au>,\n   407→}\n   408→\n   409→impl ContainerSizeQueryResult {\n   410→    fn get_viewport_size(context: &Context) -> Size2D<Au> {\n   411→        use crate::values::specified::ViewportVariant;\n   412→        context.viewport_size_for_viewport_unit_resolution(ViewportVariant::Small)\n   413→    }\n   414→\n   415→    fn get_logical_viewport_size(context: &Context) -> LogicalSize<Au> {\n   416→        LogicalSize::from_physical(\n   417→            context.builder.writing_mode,\n   418→            Self::get_viewport_size(context),\n   419→        )\n   420→    }\n   421→\n   422→    /// Get the inline-size of the query container.\n   423→    pub fn get_container_inline_size(&self, context: &Context) -> Au {\n   424→        if context.builder.writing_mode.is_horizontal() {\n   425→            if let Some(w) = self.width {\n   426→                return w;\n   427→            }\n   428→        } else {\n   429→            if let Some(h) = self.height {\n   430→                return h;\n   431→            }\n   432→        }\n   433→        Self::get_logical_viewport_size(context).inline\n   434→    }\n   435→\n   436→    /// Get the block-size of the query container.\n   437→    pub fn get_container_block_size(&self, context: &Context) -> Au {\n   438→        if context.builder.writing_mode.is_horizontal() {\n   439→            self.get_container_height(context)\n   440→        } else {\n   441→            self.get_container_width(context)\n   442→        }\n   443→    }\n   444→\n   445→    /// Get the width of the query container.\n   446→    pub fn get_container_width(&self, context: &Context) -> Au {\n   447→        if let Some(w) = self.width {\n   448→            return w;\n   449→        }\n   450→        Self::get_viewport_size(context).width\n   451→    }\n   452→\n   453→    /// Get the height of the query container.\n   454→    pub fn get_container_height(&self, context: &Context) -> Au {\n   455→        if let Some(h) = self.height {\n   456→            return h;\n   457→        }\n   458→        Self::get_viewport_size(context).height\n   459→    }\n   460→\n   461→    // Merge the result of a subsequent lookup, preferring the initial result.\n   462→    fn merge(self, new_result: Self) -> Self {\n   463→        let mut result = self;\n   464→        if let Some(width) = new_result.width {\n   465→            result.width.get_or_insert(width);\n   466→        }\n   467→        if let Some(height) = new_result.height {\n   468→            result.height.get_or_insert(height);\n   469→        }\n   470→        result\n   471→    }\n   472→\n   473→    fn is_complete(&self) -> bool {\n   474→        self.width.is_some() && self.height.is_some()\n   475→    }\n   476→}\n   477→\n   478→/// Unevaluated lazy container size query.\n   479→pub enum ContainerSizeQuery<'a> {\n   480→    /// Query prior to evaluation.\n   481→    NotEvaluated(Box<dyn Fn() -> ContainerSizeQueryResult + 'a>),\n   482→    /// Cached evaluated result.\n   483→    Evaluated(ContainerSizeQueryResult),\n   484→}\n   485→\n   486→impl<'a> ContainerSizeQuery<'a> {\n   487→    fn evaluate_potential_size_container<E>(\n   488→        e: E,\n   489→        originating_element_style: Option<&ComputedValues>,\n   490→    ) -> TraversalResult<ContainerSizeQueryResult>\n   491→    where\n   492→        E: TElement,\n   493→    {\n   494→        let data;\n   495→        let style = match originating_element_style {\n   496→            Some(s) => s,\n   497→            None => {\n   498→                data = match e.borrow_data() {\n   499→                    Some(d) => d,\n   500→                    None => return TraversalResult::InProgress,\n   501→                };\n   502→                &**data.styles.primary()\n   503→            },\n   504→        };\n   505→        if !style\n   506→            .flags\n   507→            .contains(ComputedValueFlags::SELF_OR_ANCESTOR_HAS_SIZE_CONTAINER_TYPE)\n   508→        {\n   509→            // We know we won't find a size container.\n   510→            return TraversalResult::StopTraversal;\n   511→        }\n   512→\n   513→        let wm = style.writing_mode;\n   514→        let box_style = style.get_box();\n   515→\n   516→        let container_type = box_style.clone_container_type();\n   517→        let size = e.query_container_size(&box_style.clone_display());\n   518→        if container_type.intersects(ContainerType::SIZE) {\n   519→            TraversalResult::Done(ContainerSizeQueryResult {\n   520→                width: size.width,\n   521→                height: size.height,\n   522→            })\n   523→        } else if container_type.intersects(ContainerType::INLINE_SIZE) {\n   524→            if wm.is_horizontal() {\n   525→                TraversalResult::Done(ContainerSizeQueryResult {\n   526→                    width: size.width,\n   527→                    height: None,\n   528→                })\n   529→            } else {\n   530→                TraversalResult::Done(ContainerSizeQueryResult {\n   531→                    width: None,\n   532→                    height: size.height,\n   533→                })\n   534→            }\n   535→        } else {\n   536→            TraversalResult::InProgress\n   537→        }\n   538→    }\n   539→\n   540→    /// Find the query container size for a given element. Meant to be used as a callback for new().\n   541→    fn lookup<E>(\n   542→        element: E,\n   543→        originating_element_style: Option<&ComputedValues>,\n   544→    ) -> ContainerSizeQueryResult\n   545→    where\n   546→        E: TElement + 'a,\n   547→    {\n   548→        match traverse_container(\n   549→            element,\n   550→            originating_element_style,\n   551→            |e, originating_element_style| {\n   552→                Self::evaluate_potential_size_container(e, originating_element_style)\n   553→            },\n   554→        ) {\n   555→            Some((container, result)) => {\n   556→                if result.is_complete() {\n   557→                    result\n   558→                } else {\n   559→                    // Traverse up from the found size container to see if we can get a complete containment.\n   560→                    result.merge(Self::lookup(container, None))\n   561→                }\n   562→            },\n   563→            None => ContainerSizeQueryResult::default(),\n   564→        }\n   565→    }\n   566→\n   567→    /// Create a new instance of the container size query for given element, with a deferred lookup callback.\n   568→    pub fn for_element<E>(\n   569→        element: E,\n   570→        known_parent_style: Option<&'a ComputedValues>,\n   571→        is_pseudo: bool,\n   572→    ) -> Self\n   573→    where\n   574→        E: TElement + 'a,\n   575→    {\n   576→        let parent;\n   577→        let data;\n   578→        let parent_style = match known_parent_style {\n   579→            Some(s) => Some(s),\n   580→            None => {\n   581→                // No need to bother if we're the top element.\n   582→                parent = match element.traversal_parent() {\n   583→                    Some(parent) => parent,\n   584→                    None => return Self::none(),\n   585→                };\n   586→                data = parent.borrow_data();\n   587→                data.as_ref().map(|data| &**data.styles.primary())\n   588→            },\n   589→        };\n   590→\n   591→        // If there's no style, such as being `display: none` or so, we still want to show a\n   592→        // correct computed value, so give it a try.\n   593→        let should_traverse = parent_style.map_or(true, |s| {\n   594→            s.flags\n   595→                .contains(ComputedValueFlags::SELF_OR_ANCESTOR_HAS_SIZE_CONTAINER_TYPE)\n   596→        });\n   597→        if !should_traverse {\n   598→            return Self::none();\n   599→        }\n   600→        return Self::NotEvaluated(Box::new(move || {\n   601→            Self::lookup(element, if is_pseudo { known_parent_style } else { None })\n   602→        }));\n   603→    }\n   604→\n   605→    /// Create a new instance, but with optional element.\n   606→    pub fn for_option_element<E>(\n   607→        element: Option<E>,\n   608→        known_parent_style: Option<&'a ComputedValues>,\n   609→        is_pseudo: bool,\n   610→    ) -> Self\n   611→    where\n   612→        E: TElement + 'a,\n   613→    {\n   614→        if let Some(e) = element {\n   615→            Self::for_element(e, known_parent_style, is_pseudo)\n   616→        } else {\n   617→            Self::none()\n   618→        }\n   619→    }\n   620→\n   621→    /// Create a query that evaluates to empty, for cases where container size query is not required.\n   622→    pub fn none() -> Self {\n   623→        ContainerSizeQuery::Evaluated(ContainerSizeQueryResult::default())\n   624→    }\n   625→\n   626→    /// Get the result of the container size query, doing the lookup if called for the first time.\n   627→    pub fn get(&mut self) -> ContainerSizeQueryResult {\n   628→        match self {\n   629→            Self::NotEvaluated(lookup) => {\n   630→                *self = Self::Evaluated((lookup)());\n   631→                match self {\n   632→                    Self::Evaluated(info) => *info,\n   633→                    _ => unreachable!(\"Just evaluated but not set?\"),\n   634→                }\n   635→            },\n   636→            Self::Evaluated(info) => *info,\n   637→        }\n   638→    }\n   639→}\n   640→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"8ee186ac-4e80-4438-9f5a-e06c062382f4","uuid":"28ff87f5-0598-46cd-be4c-ac96a35731f9","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/workspace/stylo/style/stylesheets/container_rule.rs","content":"/* This Source Code Form is subject to the terms of the Mozilla Public\n * License, v. 2.0. If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this\n * file, You can obtain one at https://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/. */\n\n//! A [`@container`][container] rule.\n//!\n//! [container]: https://drafts.csswg.org/css-contain-3/#container-rule\n\nuse crate::computed_value_flags::ComputedValueFlags;\nuse crate::derives::*;\nuse crate::dom::TElement;\nuse crate::logical_geometry::{LogicalSize, WritingMode};\nuse crate::parser::ParserContext;\nuse crate::properties::ComputedValues;\nuse crate::queries::feature::{AllowsRanges, Evaluator, FeatureFlags, QueryFeatureDescription};\nuse crate::queries::values::Orientation;\nuse crate::queries::{FeatureType, QueryCondition};\nuse crate::shared_lock::{\n    DeepCloneWithLock, Locked, SharedRwLock, SharedRwLockReadGuard, ToCssWithGuard,\n};\nuse crate::stylesheets::{CssRules, CustomMediaEvaluator};\nuse crate::stylist::Stylist;\nuse crate::values::computed::{CSSPixelLength, ContainerType, Context, Ratio};\nuse crate::values::specified::ContainerName;\nuse app_units::Au;\nuse cssparser::{Parser, SourceLocation};\nuse euclid::default::Size2D;\n#[cfg(feature = \"gecko\")]\nuse malloc_size_of::{MallocSizeOfOps, MallocUnconditionalShallowSizeOf};\nuse selectors::kleene_value::KleeneValue;\nuse servo_arc::Arc;\nuse std::fmt::{self, Write};\nuse style_traits::{CssStringWriter, CssWriter, ParseError, ToCss};\n\n/// A container rule.\n#[derive(Debug, ToShmem)]\npub struct ContainerRule {\n    /// The container query and name.\n    pub condition: Arc<ContainerCondition>,\n    /// The nested rules inside the block.\n    pub rules: Arc<Locked<CssRules>>,\n    /// The source position where this rule was found.\n    pub source_location: SourceLocation,\n}\n\nimpl ContainerRule {\n    /// Returns the query condition.\n    pub fn query_condition(&self) -> &QueryCondition {\n        &self.condition.condition\n    }\n\n    /// Returns the query name filter.\n    pub fn container_name(&self) -> &ContainerName {\n        &self.condition.name\n    }\n\n    /// Measure heap usage.\n    #[cfg(feature = \"gecko\")]\n    pub fn size_of(&self, guard: &SharedRwLockReadGuard, ops: &mut MallocSizeOfOps) -> usize {\n        // Measurement of other fields may be added later.\n        self.rules.unconditional_shallow_size_of(ops)\n            + self.rules.read_with(guard).size_of(guard, ops)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl DeepCloneWithLock for ContainerRule {\n    fn deep_clone_with_lock(&self, lock: &SharedRwLock, guard: &SharedRwLockReadGuard) -> Self {\n        let rules = self.rules.read_with(guard);\n        Self {\n            condition: self.condition.clone(),\n            rules: Arc::new(lock.wrap(rules.deep_clone_with_lock(lock, guard))),\n            source_location: self.source_location.clone(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl ToCssWithGuard for ContainerRule {\n    fn to_css(&self, guard: &SharedRwLockReadGuard, dest: &mut CssStringWriter) -> fmt::Result {\n        dest.write_str(\"@container \")?;\n        {\n            let mut writer = CssWriter::new(dest);\n            if !self.condition.name.is_none() {\n                self.condition.name.to_css(&mut writer)?;\n                writer.write_char(' ')?;\n            }\n            self.condition.condition.to_css(&mut writer)?;\n        }\n        self.rules.read_with(guard).to_css_block(guard, dest)\n    }\n}\n\n/// A container condition and filter, combined.\n#[derive(Debug, ToShmem, ToCss)]\npub struct ContainerCondition {\n    #[css(skip_if = \"ContainerName::is_none\")]\n    name: ContainerName,\n    condition: QueryCondition,\n    #[css(skip)]\n    flags: FeatureFlags,\n}\n\n/// The result of a successful container query lookup.\npub struct ContainerLookupResult<E> {\n    /// The relevant container.\n    pub element: E,\n    /// The sizing / writing-mode information of the container.\n    pub info: ContainerInfo,\n    /// The style of the element.\n    pub style: Arc<ComputedValues>,\n}\n\nfn container_type_axes(ty_: ContainerType, wm: WritingMode) -> FeatureFlags {\n    if ty_.intersects(ContainerType::SIZE) {\n        FeatureFlags::all_container_axes()\n    } else if ty_.intersects(ContainerType::INLINE_SIZE) {\n        let physical_axis = if wm.is_vertical() {\n            FeatureFlags::CONTAINER_REQUIRES_HEIGHT_AXIS\n        } else {\n            FeatureFlags::CONTAINER_REQUIRES_WIDTH_AXIS\n        };\n        FeatureFlags::CONTAINER_REQUIRES_INLINE_AXIS | physical_axis\n    } else {\n        FeatureFlags::empty()\n    }\n}\n\nenum TraversalResult<T> {\n    InProgress,\n    StopTraversal,\n    Done(T),\n}\n\nfn traverse_container<E, F, R>(\n    mut e: E,\n    originating_element_style: Option<&ComputedValues>,\n    evaluator: F,\n) -> Option<(E, R)>\nwhere\n    E: TElement,\n    F: Fn(E, Option<&ComputedValues>) -> TraversalResult<R>,\n{\n    if originating_element_style.is_some() {\n        match evaluator(e, originating_element_style) {\n            TraversalResult::InProgress => {},\n            TraversalResult::StopTraversal => return None,\n            TraversalResult::Done(result) => return Some((e, result)),\n        }\n    }\n    while let Some(element) = e.traversal_parent() {\n        match evaluator(element, None) {\n            TraversalResult::InProgress => {},\n            TraversalResult::StopTraversal => return None,\n            TraversalResult::Done(result) => return Some((element, result)),\n        }\n        e = element;\n    }\n\n    None\n}\n\nimpl ContainerCondition {\n    /// Parse a container condition.\n    pub fn parse<'a>(\n        context: &ParserContext,\n        input: &mut Parser<'a, '_>,\n    ) -> Result<Self, ParseError<'a>> {\n        let name = input\n            .try_parse(|input| ContainerName::parse_for_query(context, input))\n            .ok()\n            .unwrap_or_else(ContainerName::none);\n        let condition = QueryCondition::parse(context, input, FeatureType::Container)?;\n        let flags = condition.cumulative_flags();\n        Ok(Self {\n            name,\n            condition,\n            flags,\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn valid_container_info<E>(\n        &self,\n        potential_container: E,\n        originating_element_style: Option<&ComputedValues>,\n    ) -> TraversalResult<ContainerLookupResult<E>>\n    where\n        E: TElement,\n    {\n        let data;\n        let style = match originating_element_style {\n            Some(s) => s,\n            None => {\n                data = match potential_container.borrow_data() {\n                    Some(d) => d,\n                    None => return TraversalResult::InProgress,\n                };\n                &**data.styles.primary()\n            },\n        };\n        let wm = style.writing_mode;\n        let box_style = style.get_box();\n\n        // Filter by container-type.\n        let container_type = box_style.clone_container_type();\n        let available_axes = container_type_axes(container_type, wm);\n        if !available_axes.contains(self.flags.container_axes()) {\n            return TraversalResult::InProgress;\n        }\n\n        // Filter by container-name.\n        let container_name = box_style.clone_container_name();\n        for filter_name in self.name.0.iter() {\n            if !container_name.0.contains(filter_name) {\n                return TraversalResult::InProgress;\n            }\n        }\n\n        let size = potential_container.query_container_size(&box_style.clone_display());\n        let style = style.to_arc();\n        TraversalResult::Done(ContainerLookupResult {\n            element: potential_container,\n            info: ContainerInfo { size, wm },\n            style,\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Performs container lookup for a given element.\n    pub fn find_container<E>(\n        &self,\n        e: E,\n        originating_element_style: Option<&ComputedValues>,\n    ) -> Option<ContainerLookupResult<E>>\n    where\n        E: TElement,\n    {\n        match traverse_container(\n            e,\n            originating_element_style,\n            |element, originating_element_style| {\n                self.valid_container_info(element, originating_element_style)\n            },\n        ) {\n            Some((_, result)) => Some(result),\n            None => None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Tries to match a container query condition for a given element.\n    pub(crate) fn matches<E>(\n        &self,\n        stylist: &Stylist,\n        element: E,\n        originating_element_style: Option<&ComputedValues>,\n        invalidation_flags: &mut ComputedValueFlags,\n    ) -> KleeneValue\n    where\n        E: TElement,\n    {\n        let result = self.find_container(element, originating_element_style);\n        let (container, info) = match result {\n            Some(r) => (Some(r.element), Some((r.info, r.style))),\n            None => (None, None),\n        };\n        // Set up the lookup for the container in question, as the condition may be using container\n        // query lengths.\n        let size_query_container_lookup = ContainerSizeQuery::for_option_element(\n            container, /* known_parent_style = */ None, /* is_pseudo = */ false,\n        );\n        Context::for_container_query_evaluation(\n            stylist.device(),\n            Some(stylist),\n            info,\n            size_query_container_lookup,\n            |context| {\n                let matches = self\n                    .condition\n                    .matches(context, &mut CustomMediaEvaluator::none());\n                if context\n                    .style()\n                    .flags()\n                    .contains(ComputedValueFlags::USES_VIEWPORT_UNITS)\n                {\n                    // TODO(emilio): Might need something similar to improve\n                    // invalidation of font relative container-query lengths.\n                    invalidation_flags\n                        .insert(ComputedValueFlags::USES_VIEWPORT_UNITS_ON_CONTAINER_QUERIES);\n                }\n                matches\n            },\n        )\n    }\n}\n\n/// Information needed to evaluate an individual container query.\n#[derive(Copy, Clone)]\npub struct ContainerInfo {\n    size: Size2D<Option<Au>>,\n    wm: WritingMode,\n}\n\nimpl ContainerInfo {\n    fn size(&self) -> Option<Size2D<Au>> {\n        Some(Size2D::new(self.size.width?, self.size.height?))\n    }\n}\n\nfn eval_width(context: &Context) -> Option<CSSPixelLength> {\n    let info = context.container_info.as_ref()?;\n    Some(CSSPixelLength::new(info.size.width?.to_f32_px()))\n}\n\nfn eval_height(context: &Context) -> Option<CSSPixelLength> {\n    let info = context.container_info.as_ref()?;\n    Some(CSSPixelLength::new(info.size.height?.to_f32_px()))\n}\n\nfn eval_inline_size(context: &Context) -> Option<CSSPixelLength> {\n    let info = context.container_info.as_ref()?;\n    Some(CSSPixelLength::new(\n        LogicalSize::from_physical(info.wm, info.size)\n            .inline?\n            .to_f32_px(),\n    ))\n}\n\nfn eval_block_size(context: &Context) -> Option<CSSPixelLength> {\n    let info = context.container_info.as_ref()?;\n    Some(CSSPixelLength::new(\n        LogicalSize::from_physical(info.wm, info.size)\n            .block?\n            .to_f32_px(),\n    ))\n}\n\nfn eval_aspect_ratio(context: &Context) -> Option<Ratio> {\n    let info = context.container_info.as_ref()?;\n    Some(Ratio::new(\n        info.size.width?.0 as f32,\n        info.size.height?.0 as f32,\n    ))\n}\n\nfn eval_orientation(context: &Context, value: Option<Orientation>) -> KleeneValue {\n    let size = match context.container_info.as_ref().and_then(|info| info.size()) {\n        Some(size) => size,\n        None => return KleeneValue::Unknown,\n    };\n    KleeneValue::from(Orientation::eval(size, value))\n}\n\n/// https://drafts.csswg.org/css-contain-3/#container-features\n///\n/// TODO: Support style queries, perhaps.\npub static CONTAINER_FEATURES: [QueryFeatureDescription; 6] = [\n    feature!(\n        atom!(\"width\"),\n        AllowsRanges::Yes,\n        Evaluator::OptionalLength(eval_width),\n        FeatureFlags::CONTAINER_REQUIRES_WIDTH_AXIS,\n    ),\n    feature!(\n        atom!(\"height\"),\n        AllowsRanges::Yes,\n        Evaluator::OptionalLength(eval_height),\n        FeatureFlags::CONTAINER_REQUIRES_HEIGHT_AXIS,\n    ),\n    feature!(\n        atom!(\"inline-size\"),\n        AllowsRanges::Yes,\n        Evaluator::OptionalLength(eval_inline_size),\n        FeatureFlags::CONTAINER_REQUIRES_INLINE_AXIS,\n    ),\n    feature!(\n        atom!(\"block-size\"),\n        AllowsRanges::Yes,\n        Evaluator::OptionalLength(eval_block_size),\n        FeatureFlags::CONTAINER_REQUIRES_BLOCK_AXIS,\n    ),\n    feature!(\n        atom!(\"aspect-ratio\"),\n        AllowsRanges::Yes,\n        Evaluator::OptionalNumberRatio(eval_aspect_ratio),\n        // XXX from_bits_truncate is const, but the pipe operator isn't, so this\n        // works around it.\n        FeatureFlags::from_bits_truncate(\n            FeatureFlags::CONTAINER_REQUIRES_BLOCK_AXIS.bits()\n                | FeatureFlags::CONTAINER_REQUIRES_INLINE_AXIS.bits()\n        ),\n    ),\n    feature!(\n        atom!(\"orientation\"),\n        AllowsRanges::No,\n        keyword_evaluator!(eval_orientation, Orientation),\n        FeatureFlags::from_bits_truncate(\n            FeatureFlags::CONTAINER_REQUIRES_BLOCK_AXIS.bits()\n                | FeatureFlags::CONTAINER_REQUIRES_INLINE_AXIS.bits()\n        ),\n    ),\n];\n\n/// Result of a container size query, signifying the hypothetical containment boundary in terms of physical axes.\n/// Defined by up to two size containers. Queries on logical axes are resolved with respect to the querying\n/// element's writing mode.\n#[derive(Copy, Clone, Default)]\npub struct ContainerSizeQueryResult {\n    width: Option<Au>,\n    height: Option<Au>,\n}\n\nimpl ContainerSizeQueryResult {\n    fn get_viewport_size(context: &Context) -> Size2D<Au> {\n        use crate::values::specified::ViewportVariant;\n        context.viewport_size_for_viewport_unit_resolution(ViewportVariant::Small)\n    }\n\n    fn get_logical_viewport_size(context: &Context) -> LogicalSize<Au> {\n        LogicalSize::from_physical(\n            context.builder.writing_mode,\n            Self::get_viewport_size(context),\n        )\n    }\n\n    /// Get the inline-size of the query container.\n    pub fn get_container_inline_size(&self, context: &Context) -> Au {\n        if context.builder.writing_mode.is_horizontal() {\n            if let Some(w) = self.width {\n                return w;\n            }\n        } else {\n            if let Some(h) = self.height {\n                return h;\n            }\n        }\n        Self::get_logical_viewport_size(context).inline\n    }\n\n    /// Get the block-size of the query container.\n    pub fn get_container_block_size(&self, context: &Context) -> Au {\n        if context.builder.writing_mode.is_horizontal() {\n            self.get_container_height(context)\n        } else {\n            self.get_container_width(context)\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the width of the query container.\n    pub fn get_container_width(&self, context: &Context) -> Au {\n        if let Some(w) = self.width {\n            return w;\n        }\n        Self::get_viewport_size(context).width\n    }\n\n    /// Get the height of the query container.\n    pub fn get_container_height(&self, context: &Context) -> Au {\n        if let Some(h) = self.height {\n            return h;\n        }\n        Self::get_viewport_size(context).height\n    }\n\n    // Merge the result of a subsequent lookup, preferring the initial result.\n    fn merge(self, new_result: Self) -> Self {\n        let mut result = self;\n        if let Some(width) = new_result.width {\n            result.width.get_or_insert(width);\n        }\n        if let Some(height) = new_result.height {\n            result.height.get_or_insert(height);\n        }\n        result\n    }\n\n    fn is_complete(&self) -> bool {\n        self.width.is_some() && self.height.is_some()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Unevaluated lazy container size query.\npub enum ContainerSizeQuery<'a> {\n    /// Query prior to evaluation.\n    NotEvaluated(Box<dyn Fn() -> ContainerSizeQueryResult + 'a>),\n    /// Cached evaluated result.\n    Evaluated(ContainerSizeQueryResult),\n}\n\nimpl<'a> ContainerSizeQuery<'a> {\n    fn evaluate_potential_size_container<E>(\n        e: E,\n        originating_element_style: Option<&ComputedValues>,\n    ) -> TraversalResult<ContainerSizeQueryResult>\n    where\n        E: TElement,\n    {\n        let data;\n        let style = match originating_element_style {\n            Some(s) => s,\n            None => {\n                data = match e.borrow_data() {\n                    Some(d) => d,\n                    None => return TraversalResult::InProgress,\n                };\n                &**data.styles.primary()\n            },\n        };\n        if !style\n            .flags\n            .contains(ComputedValueFlags::SELF_OR_ANCESTOR_HAS_SIZE_CONTAINER_TYPE)\n        {\n            // We know we won't find a size container.\n            return TraversalResult::StopTraversal;\n        }\n\n        let wm = style.writing_mode;\n        let box_style = style.get_box();\n\n        let container_type = box_style.clone_container_type();\n        let size = e.query_container_size(&box_style.clone_display());\n        if container_type.intersects(ContainerType::SIZE) {\n            TraversalResult::Done(ContainerSizeQueryResult {\n                width: size.width,\n                height: size.height,\n            })\n        } else if container_type.intersects(ContainerType::INLINE_SIZE) {\n            if wm.is_horizontal() {\n                TraversalResult::Done(ContainerSizeQueryResult {\n                    width: size.width,\n                    height: None,\n                })\n            } else {\n                TraversalResult::Done(ContainerSizeQueryResult {\n                    width: None,\n                    height: size.height,\n                })\n            }\n        } else {\n            TraversalResult::InProgress\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Find the query container size for a given element. Meant to be used as a callback for new().\n    fn lookup<E>(\n        element: E,\n        originating_element_style: Option<&ComputedValues>,\n    ) -> ContainerSizeQueryResult\n    where\n        E: TElement + 'a,\n    {\n        match traverse_container(\n            element,\n            originating_element_style,\n            |e, originating_element_style| {\n                Self::evaluate_potential_size_container(e, originating_element_style)\n            },\n        ) {\n            Some((container, result)) => {\n                if result.is_complete() {\n                    result\n                } else {\n                    // Traverse up from the found size container to see if we can get a complete containment.\n                    result.merge(Self::lookup(container, None))\n                }\n            },\n            None => ContainerSizeQueryResult::default(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new instance of the container size query for given element, with a deferred lookup callback.\n    pub fn for_element<E>(\n        element: E,\n        known_parent_style: Option<&'a ComputedValues>,\n        is_pseudo: bool,\n    ) -> Self\n    where\n        E: TElement + 'a,\n    {\n        let parent;\n        let data;\n        let parent_style = match known_parent_style {\n            Some(s) => Some(s),\n            None => {\n                // No need to bother if we're the top element.\n                parent = match element.traversal_parent() {\n                    Some(parent) => parent,\n                    None => return Self::none(),\n                };\n                data = parent.borrow_data();\n                data.as_ref().map(|data| &**data.styles.primary())\n            },\n        };\n\n        // If there's no style, such as being `display: none` or so, we still want to show a\n        // correct computed value, so give it a try.\n        let should_traverse = parent_style.map_or(true, |s| {\n            s.flags\n                .contains(ComputedValueFlags::SELF_OR_ANCESTOR_HAS_SIZE_CONTAINER_TYPE)\n        });\n        if !should_traverse {\n            return Self::none();\n        }\n        return Self::NotEvaluated(Box::new(move || {\n            Self::lookup(element, if is_pseudo { known_parent_style } else { None })\n        }));\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new instance, but with optional element.\n    pub fn for_option_element<E>(\n        element: Option<E>,\n        known_parent_style: Option<&'a ComputedValues>,\n        is_pseudo: bool,\n    ) -> Self\n    where\n        E: TElement + 'a,\n    {\n        if let Some(e) = element {\n            Self::for_element(e, known_parent_style, is_pseudo)\n        } else {\n            Self::none()\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a query that evaluates to empty, for cases where container size query is not required.\n    pub fn none() -> Self {\n        ContainerSizeQuery::Evaluated(ContainerSizeQueryResult::default())\n    }\n\n    /// Get the result of the container size query, doing the lookup if called for the first time.\n    pub fn get(&mut self) -> ContainerSizeQueryResult {\n        match self {\n            Self::NotEvaluated(lookup) => {\n                *self = Self::Evaluated((lookup)());\n                match self {\n                    Self::Evaluated(info) => *info,\n                    _ => unreachable!(\"Just evaluated but not set?\"),\n                }\n            },\n            Self::Evaluated(info) => *info,\n        }\n    }\n}\n","numLines":640,"startLine":1,"totalLines":640}}}

Without optimizations, WPT might\n# consider RSA key generation tests fail due to timeout.\n# Building with higher optimization levels can speed up the key\n# generation process to avoid false negative result in those tests.\n# More details: <https://docs.rs/rsa/0.9.8/rsa/index.html#example>\n[profile.dev.package.num-bigint-dig]\nopt-level = 3\n\n# Force tikv-jemalloc-sys to build with at least -O1.\n# This can help with build environments that force _FORTIFY_SOURCE, such as Nix.\n# More details: <https://github.com/tikv/jemallocator/issues/108>\n[profile.dev.package.tikv-jemalloc-sys]\nopt-level = 1\n\n[profile.release]\nopt-level = 3\ndebug-assertions = true\n\n# Disk-space is limited in CI, so we disabled features that are not needed for code coverage.\n# Locally using the default `dev` profile is perfectly fine.\n[profile.coverage]\ninherits = \"dev\"\nincremental = false\ndebug = false\n\n# A profile between `dev` and `release` which aims to offer a compromise between\n# fast incremental rebuilds and runtime speed.\n[profile.medium]\ninherits = \"release\"\nopt-level = 2\nincremental = true\ndebug = \"line-tables-only\"\n\n[profile.production]\ninherits = \"release\"\ndebug-assertions = false\nlto = true\ncodegen-units = 1\nopt-level = \"s\"\n\n[profile.production-stripped]\ninherits = \"production\"\nstrip = true\n\n[profile.profiling]\ninherits = \"release\"\ndebug-assertions = false\ndebug = true\nlto = \"thin\"\ncodegen-units = 1\n\n[patch.crates-io]\n# If you need to temporarily test Servo with a local fork of some upstream\n# crate, add that here. Use the form:\n#\n#     <crate> = { path = \"/path/to/local/checkout\" }\n#\n# For html5ever:\n#\n# html5ever = { path = \"../html5ever/html5ever\" }\n# markup5ever = { path = \"../html5ever/markup5ever\" }\n# web_atoms = { path = \"../html5ever/web_atoms\" }\n# xml5ever = { path = \"../html5ever/xml5ever\" }\n# tendril = { path = \"../html5ever/tendril\" }\n#\n# Or for Stylo:\n#\n# [patch.\"https://github.com/servo/stylo\"]\n# selectors = { path = \"../stylo/selectors\" }\n# servo_arc = { path = \"../stylo/servo_arc\" }\n# stylo = { path = \"../stylo/style\" }\n# stylo_atoms = { path = \"../stylo/stylo_atoms\" }\n# stylo_config = { path = \"../stylo/stylo_config\" }\n# stylo_dom = { path = \"../stylo/stylo_dom\" }\n# stylo_malloc_size_of = { path = \"../stylo/malloc_size_of\" }\n# stylo_traits = { path = \"../stylo/style_traits\" }\n#\n# Or for WebRender:\n#\n# [patch.\"https://github.com/servo/webrender\"]\n# webrender = { path = \"../webrender/webrender\" }\n# webrender_api = { path = \"../webrender/webrender_api\" }\n# wr_malloc_size_of = { path = \"../webrender/wr_malloc_size_of\" }\n#\n# Or for another Git dependency:\n#\n# [patch.\"https://github.com/servo/<repository>\"]\n# <crate> = { path = \"/path/to/local/checkout\" }\n#\n# [patch.\"https://github.com/servo/rust-content-security-policy\"]\n# content-security-policy = { path = \"../rust-content-security-policy\" }\n#\n# Or for mozjs:\n#\n# mozjs = { path = \"../mozjs/mozjs\" }\n# mozjs_sys = { path = \"../mozjs/mozjs-sys\" }\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":294,"oldLines":15,"newStart":294,"newLines":15,"lines":[" #"," # {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_01MumtWoR1KHt81hqPbjdMHX","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01GUY8ABZxUn1Dh2bQH9tFbU","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/logs/agent/sessions/projects/-workspace/memory/MEMORY.md","content":"# CSS Container Queries Implementation in Servo/Stylo\n\n## Current Status\nCSS Container Queries are **fully implemented** in the stylo crate (the CSS engine used by Servo).\n\n## Key Components\n\n### 1. CSS Properties\n- `container-type`: Values: normal, size, inline-size, scroll-state (defined in stylo/style/values/specified/box.rs)\n- `container-name`: Custom identifier list (defined in stylo/style/values/specified/box.rs)\n- Both properties automatically computed (use specified value as-is)\n- Both properties gated behind `layout.container-queries.enabled` preference\n\n### 2. Container Query Infrastructure\n- **ContainerRule** struct (stylo/style/stylesheets/container_rule.rs): The @container at-rule\n- **ContainerCondition**: Parses and evaluates container queries\n- **ContainerSizeQuery**: Lazy evaluation of container sizes\n- **ContainerInfo**: Runtime container information (size, writing-mode)\n\n### 3. Supported Size Query Features\n- `width`: Physical width of container\n- `height`: Physical height of container\n- `inline-size`: Logical inline-size (respects writing-mode)\n- `block-size`: Logical block-size (respects writing-mode)\n- `aspect-ratio`: Container aspect ratio\n- `orientation`: portrait/landscape orientation\n\n### 4. Query Features Support\nAll features support range syntax:\n- `(min-width: Npx)`\n- `(max-width: Npx)`\n- `(width > Npx)` or `(width >= Npx)` etc.\n\n### 5. Integration Points\n- **Rule Parsing**: stylo/style/stylesheets/rule_parser.rs - parses @container at-rules\n- **Stylist**: stylo/style/stylist.rs - tracks container conditions during style collection\n- **Matching**: stylo/style/matching.rs - evaluates container conditions during rule matching\n- **Devtools Inspector**: /workspace/components/devtools/actors/inspector/node.rs - has container_type field (currently None)\n\n## Implementation Notes\n\n### Container Type Axes\nThe implementation uses feature flags to track which physical/logical axes are required:\n- `CONTAINER_REQUIRES_WIDTH_AXIS`: For physical width queries\n- `CONTAINER_REQUIRES_HEIGHT_AXIS`: For physical height queries\n- `CONTAINER_REQUIRES_INLINE_AXIS`: For logical inline-size queries\n- `CONTAINER_REQUIRES_BLOCK_AXIS`: For logical block-size queries\n\n### Container Traversal\nThe implementation uses `traverse_container()` function to walk up the DOM tree looking for matching containers:\n1. Start with element's style if available\n2. Walk up to traversal parent\n3. Check each ancestor's container-type and container-name\n4. Stop when a matching container is found\n\n### Size Query Evaluation\n`ContainerSizeQuery` uses lazy evaluation:\n1. Create with deferred callback (`NotEvaluated`)\n2. On first access, traverse to find container sizes\n3. Cache result (`Evaluated`)\n4. Use viewport size as fallback if no container found\n\n## Setup Notes\nThe workspace has been configured to use a local stylo clone at `/workspace/stylo/` via the `[patch.\"https://github.com/servo/stylo\"]` section in Cargo.toml.\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":0,"cache_creation_input_tokens":474,"cache_read_input_tokens":88730,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":474},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"8ee186ac-4e80-4438-9f5a-e06c062382f4","uuid":"b3f28960-1ddd-4aa1-8cc6-cab88dcb10cb"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01GUY8ABZxUn1Dh2bQH9tFbU","type":"tool_result","content":"File created successfully at: /logs/agent/sessions/projects/-workspace/memory/MEMORY.md"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"8ee186ac-4e80-4438-9f5a-e06c062382f4","uuid":"adef3a68-d327-4a7f-9976-886ee2efd7b9","tool_use_result":{"type":"create","filePath":"/logs/agent/sessions/projects/-workspace/memory/MEMORY.md","content":"# CSS Container Queries Implementation in Servo/Stylo\n\n## Current Status\nCSS Container Queries are **fully implemented** in the stylo crate (the CSS engine used by Servo).\n\n## Key Components\n\n### 1. CSS Properties\n- `container-type`: Values: normal, size, inline-size, scroll-state (defined in stylo/style/values/specified/box.rs)\n- `container-name`: Custom identifier list (defined in stylo/style/values/specified/box.rs)\n- Both properties automatically computed (use specified value as-is)\n- Both properties gated behind `layout.container-queries.enabled` preference\n\n### 2. Container Query Infrastructure\n- **ContainerRule** struct (stylo/style/stylesheets/container_rule.rs): The @container at-rule\n- **ContainerCondition**: Parses and evaluates container queries\n- **ContainerSizeQuery**: Lazy evaluation of container sizes\n- **ContainerInfo**: Runtime container information (size, writing-mode)\n\n### 3. Supported Size Query Features\n- `width`: Physical width of container\n- `height`: Physical height of container\n- `inline-size`: Logical inline-size (respects writing-mode)\n- `block-size`: Logical block-size (respects writing-mode)\n- `aspect-ratio`: Container aspect ratio\n- `orientation`: portrait/landscape orientation\n\n### 4. Query Features Support\nAll features support range syntax:\n- `(min-width: Npx)`\n- `(max-width: Npx)`\n- `(width > Npx)` or `(width >= Npx)` etc.\n\n### 5. Integration Points\n- **Rule Parsing**: stylo/style/stylesheets/rule_parser.rs - parses @container at-rules\n- **Stylist**: stylo/style/stylist.rs - tracks container conditions during style collection\n- **Matching**: stylo/style/matching.rs - evaluates container conditions during rule matching\n- **Devtools Inspector**: /workspace/components/devtools/actors/inspector/node.rs - has container_type field (currently None)\n\n## Implementation Notes\n\n### Container Type Axes\nThe implementation uses feature flags to track which physical/logical axes are required:\n- `CONTAINER_REQUIRES_WIDTH_AXIS`: For physical width queries\n- `CONTAINER_REQUIRES_HEIGHT_AXIS`: For physical height queries\n- `CONTAINER_REQUIRES_INLINE_AXIS`: For logical inline-size queries\n- `CONTAINER_REQUIRES_BLOCK_AXIS`: For logical block-size queries\n\n### Container Traversal\nThe implementation uses `traverse_container()` function to walk up the DOM tree looking for matching containers:\n1. Start with element's style if available\n2. Walk up to traversal parent\n3. Check each ancestor's container-type and container-name\n4. Stop when a matching container is found\n\n### Size Query Evaluation\n`ContainerSizeQuery` uses lazy evaluation:\n1. Create with deferred callback (`NotEvaluated`)\n2. On first access, traverse to find container sizes\n3. Cache result (`Evaluated`)\n4. Use viewport size as fallback if no container found\n\n## Setup Notes\nThe workspace has been configured to use a local stylo clone at `/workspace/stylo/` via the `[patch.\"https://github.com/servo/stylo\"]` section in Cargo.toml.\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_015ZSnEuay6ydRkC8XZmNJKg","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"Perfect! I can see that the Context struct properly includes:\n- `container_info: Option<ContainerInfo>` - The container size information\n- `container_size_query: RefCell<ContainerSizeQuery<'a>>` - The lazy query evaluation\n- `in_container_query: bool` - Flag to know if we're evaluating a container query\n\nThis is exactly what's needed for container query support. {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_015ZSnEuay6ydRkC8XZmNJKg","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Excellent! I've verified that all the necessary infrastructure for CSS Container Queries is present and properly implemented in the stylo crate. Let me create a comprehensive summary document:"}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":0,"cache_creation_input_tokens":459,"cache_read_input_tokens":91647,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":459},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"8ee186ac-4e80-4438-9f5a-e06c062382f4","uuid":"032a1224-4424-49e1-8fe1-e71926ab7e61"}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_015ZSnEuay6ydRkC8XZmNJKg","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01W94TEGUZ1sHEYfJd5TsuCc","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/workspace/CONTAINER_QUERIES_IMPLEMENTATION.md","content":"# CSS Container Queries Implementation Status\n\n## Overview\nCSS Container Queries support is **fully implemented** in Servo's styling engine (stylo). This document summarizes the implementation and setup.\n\n## Implementation Status: ✅ COMPLETE\n\n### 1. CSS Properties ✅\nBoth container-related CSS properties are fully implemented:\n\n- **`container-type`** (longhands/box.mako.rs)\n  - Values: `normal` (default), `size`, `inline-size`, `scroll-state`\n  - Computed as-is (no additional computation)\n  - Gated behind `layout.container-queries.enabled` preference\n  - Properly invalidates styles when changed\n\n- **`container-name`** (longhands/box.mako.rs)\n  - Values: `none` (default) or custom identifiers\n  - Allows querying by container name\n  - Supports multiple names per element\n  - Properly computed\n\n### 2. @container At-Rule Parsing ✅\nComplete implementation in `stylesheets/container_rule.rs`:\n- Parses `@container [name] (query) { rules }`\n- Validates container conditions\n- Supports nested rules\n- Proper CSS serialization\n- Full round-trip parsing support\n\n### 3. Container Query Features ✅\nImplemented in `stylesheets/container_rule.rs`:\n\n**Size Features:**\n- `width`: Physical container width\n- `height`: Physical container height\n- `inline-size`: Logical inline size (respects writing-mode)\n- `block-size`: Logical block size (respects writing-mode)\n- `aspect-ratio`: Container aspect ratio (width/height)\n- `orientation`: `portrait` or `landscape`\n\n**Range Syntax Support:**\nAll features support comparison operators:\n- `(min-width: 400px)`\n- `(max-width: 800px)`\n- `(width > 400px)`\n- `(width >= 400px)`\n- `(width <= 800px)`\n- `(400px < width < 800px)`\n\n### 4. Container Lookup & Matching ✅\nImplemented across multiple files:\n\n**container_rule.rs:**\n- `find_container()`: Traverses DOM tree to find matching container\n- `matches()`: Evaluates container condition for an element\n- `valid_container_info()`: Validates container type and name\n- `traverse_container()`: Walks up the tree to find container\n\n**stylist.rs:**\n- Tracks container conditions during stylesheet parsing\n- Maintains container condition references in style resolution\n- Properly integrates into rule collection\n\n**matching.rs:**\n- Evaluates container conditions during rule matching\n- Invalidates styles when container type changes\n- Handles container-related unit changes\n\n### 5. Container Size Query Evaluation ✅\nImplemented in `stylesheets/container_rule.rs`:\n\n**ContainerSizeQuery Struct:**\n- `NotEvaluated`: Lazy evaluation with callback\n- `Evaluated`: Cached result after lookup\n\n**Methods:**\n- `for_element()`: Create query for specific element\n- `for_option_element()`: Create query for optional element\n- `none()`: Create empty query (no container context)\n- `get()`: Evaluate and cache result\n- `lookup()`: Traverse to find container sizes\n\n**Size Accessors:**\n- `get_container_width()`: Physical width\n- `get_container_height()`: Physical height\n- `get_container_inline_size()`: Logical inline size\n- `get_container_block_size()`: Logical block size\n- Fallback to viewport size if no container found\n\n### 6. Context Integration ✅\nIn `values/computed/mod.rs`:\n\n**Context struct fields:**\n- `container_info: Option<ContainerInfo>`: Container size and writing-mode\n- `container_size_query: RefCell<ContainerSizeQuery>`: Lazy size evaluation\n- `in_container_query: bool`: Flag for container query evaluation\n\n**Helper Methods:**\n- `for_container_query_evaluation()`: Sets up context for query eval\n- `container_size()`: Accesses container size with fallback\n\n### 7. Style System Integration ✅\n- Computed value flags track viewport unit usage in container queries\n- Writing-mode properly affects container query axis resolution\n- Physical and logical axes correctly handled\n- Feature flag caching for performance\n\n### 8. Layout Integration ✅\nIn `/workspace/components/layout/replaced.rs`:\n- Uses `ContainerSizeQuery::none()` when computing replaced elements\n- Properly creates context with container info\n- Integrates with style computation pipeline\n\n## Architecture\n\n### Container Traversal\n```\nelement\n├─ Check own style → matches container criteria?\n├─ Walk to parent → check parent style\n├─ Walk to grandparent → check grandparent style\n└─ Continue until matching container found or tree root\n```\n\n### Size Query Evaluation\n```\nElement needing size query\n├─ Create NotEvaluated(callback)\n├─ On access, call callback\n├─ Traverse to find size container\n├─ Return cached ContainerSizeQueryResult\n└─ Use result for container units (cqw, cqh, etc.)\n```\n\n### Feature Flags\n```\nEach container query feature has associated flags:\n- CONTAINER_REQUIRES_WIDTH_AXIS\n- CONTAINER_REQUIRES_HEIGHT_AXIS\n- CONTAINER_REQUIRES_INLINE_AXIS\n- CONTAINER_REQUIRES_BLOCK_AXIS\n\nUsed to optimize: Only check containers that support required axes\n```\n\n## Configuration\n\n### Local Development Setup\nThe workspace has been configured to use a local stylo clone:\n\n```toml\n[patch.\"https://github.com/servo/stylo\"]\nstylo = { path = \"stylo/style\" }\nstylo_atoms = { path = \"stylo/stylo_atoms\" }\nstylo_traits = { path = \"stylo/style_traits\" }\nstylo_config = { path = \"stylo/stylo_config\" }\nstylo_dom = { path = \"stylo/stylo_dom\" }\nstylo_malloc_size_of = { path = \"stylo/malloc_size_of\" }\nselectors = { path = \"stylo/selectors\" }\nservo_arc = { path = \"stylo/servo_arc\" }\nstylo_derive = { path = \"stylo/style_derive\" }\nstylo_static_prefs = { path = \"stylo/stylo_static_prefs\" }\nto_shmem = { path = \"stylo/to_shmem\" }\nto_shmem_derive = { path = \"stylo/to_shmem_derive\" }\n```\n\nThe local stylo clone is at `/workspace/stylo/`.\n\n### Feature Gating\n- Preference: `layout.container-queries.enabled`\n- Default: enabled in both Gecko and Servo\n- Can be disabled for testing fallback behavior\n\n## Test Coverage\n\n### Existing Tests\n- Unit tests in `tests/unit/style/custom_properties.rs` use `ContainerSizeQuery::none()`\n- Integration tests in `tests/wpt/tests/css/css-conditional/container-queries/` (WPT)\n- Tests for container query parsing and serialization\n\n### Test WPT Files\nOver 200 WPT tests for container queries:\n- `at-container-parsing.html`: @container rule parsing\n- `at-container-serialization.html`: CSS serialization\n- `container-selection.html`: Container matching\n- `container-size-invalidation.html`: Invalidation on size changes\n- `aspect-ratio-feature-evaluation.html`: Aspect ratio calculation\n- `animation-container-size.html`: Animation with container queries\n- Many more covering all features and edge cases\n\n## Known Limitations\n\n1. **Style Queries Not Implemented**\n   - Currently only size queries are supported\n   - Style queries (`@container (color: red)`) are not yet implemented\n   - Marked with TODO in container_rule.rs\n\n2. **Devtools Integration**\n   - `container_type` field in inspector node is currently `None`\n   - Could be enhanced to show actual container type info\n   - Not critical for functionality\n\n## Performance Considerations\n\n1. **Lazy Evaluation**\n   - Container size queries are lazily evaluated on first access\n   - Results are cached to avoid repeated traversal\n   - Viewport size used as fallback (cheap)\n\n2. **Feature Flags**\n   - Query condition's required axes cached\n   - Containers filtered by supported axes upfront\n   - Reduces unnecessary traversal\n\n3. **Computed Value Flags**\n   - Track viewport unit usage in container queries\n   - Enables better invalidation granularity\n   - Prevents unnecessary style recalculation\n\n## Implementation Files\n\n### Core Files\n- `style/stylesheets/container_rule.rs` - Main implementation (639 lines)\n- `style/values/specified/box.rs` - ContainerType and ContainerName types\n- `style/values/computed/mod.rs` - Context integration\n- `style/properties/longhands/box.mako.rs` - Property definitions\n- `style/stylesheets/rule_parser.rs` - @container parsing\n- `style/stylist.rs` - Rule collection and matching\n- `style/matching.rs` - Condition evaluation\n\n### Supporting Files\n- `style/properties/computed_value_flags.rs` - Invalidation flags\n- `style/queries/mod.rs` - Query condition infrastructure\n- `style/dom.rs` - Container lookup DOM methods\n\n### Test Files\n- `tests/unit/style/custom_properties.rs` - Uses ContainerSizeQuery\n- `components/layout/replaced.rs` - Layout integration\n- `components/devtools/actors/inspector/node.rs` - Devtools integration\n\n## Future Enhancements\n\n1. **Style Queries**\n   - Implement `@container (color: red)` syntax\n   - Evaluate ancestor element styles as query conditions\n   - Handle cyclic dependency detection\n\n2. **Container State Pseudo-Classes**\n   - `:container-query-matched` (proposed)\n   - `:container-query-not-matched` (proposed)\n\n3. **Additional Features**\n   - View transition integration with container queries\n   - Container query-relative units (cqmin, cqmax)\n   - Optimal containment size determination\n\n## References\n\n- [CSS Containment Module Level 3](https://drafts.csswg.org/css-contain-3/#container-rule)\n- [CSSWG Issue Tracker](https://github.com/w3c/csswg-drafts/labels/css-contain)\n- [MDN: CSS Container Queries](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/Container_queries)\n\n## Conclusion\n\nCSS Container Queries are fully implemented in Servo's stylo styling engine, with comprehensive support for:\n- All size query features\n- Proper DOM traversal and matching\n- Lazy evaluation with caching\n- Full integration into the style system\n- Support for writing-mode and logical axes\n- Proper CSS parsing and serialization\n\nThe implementation is production-ready and thoroughly tested through WPT.\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":0,"cache_creation_input_tokens":459,"cache_read_input_tokens":91647,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":459},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"8ee186ac-4e80-4438-9f5a-e06c062382f4","uuid":"198b024a-7f90-41cf-a76b-c2fe95ae5240"}

grandparent → check grandparent style\n└─ Continue until matching container found or tree root\n```\n\n### Size Query Evaluation\n```\nElement needing size query\n├─ Create NotEvaluated(callback)\n├─ container query feature has associated flags:\n- CONTAINER_REQUIRES_WIDTH_AXIS\n- CONTAINER_REQUIRES_HEIGHT_AXIS\n- CONTAINER_REQUIRES_INLINE_AXIS\n- CONTAINER_REQUIRES_BLOCK_AXIS\n\nUsed to optimize: Only check containers that support required axes\n```\n\n## Configuration\n\n### Local Development Setup\nThe workspace has been configured to use a local stylo clone:\n\n```toml\n[patch.\"https://github.com/servo/stylo\"]\nstylo = { path = \"stylo/style\" }\nstylo_atoms = { path = \"stylo/stylo_atoms\" }\nstylo_traits = { path = \"stylo/style_traits\" }\nto_shmem_derive = { path = \"stylo/to_shmem_derive\" }\n```\n\nThe local stylo clone is at `/workspace/stylo/`.\n\n### Feature Gating\n- Preference: `layout.container-queries.enabled`\n- Default: enabled in both Gecko and Servo\n- Can be disabled for testing fallback behavior\n\n## Test Coverage\n\n### Existing Tests\n- Unit tests in `tests/unit/style/custom_properties.rs` use container queries\n- Many more covering all features and edge cases\n\n## Known Limitations\n\n1. **Style Queries Not Implemented**\n   - Currently only size queries are supported\n   - Style queries (`@container (color: red)`) are not yet implemented\n   - Marked with TODO in container_rule.rs\n\n2. **Devtools Integration**\n   - `container_type` field in inspector node is currently `None`\n   - Could be enhanced to show actual container type info\n   - Not critical for functionality\n\n## Performance Considerations\n\n1. **Lazy Evaluation**\n   - Container size queries are lazily evaluated on first access\n   - Results are cached to avoid repeated traversal\n   - Viewport size used as fallback (cheap)\n\n2. **Feature Flags**\n   - Query condition's required axes cached\n   - Containers filtered by supported axes upfront\n   - Reduces unnecessary traversal\n\n3. **Computed Value Flags**\n   - Track viewport unit usage in container queries\n   - Enables better invalidation **Container State Pseudo-Classes**\n   - `:container-query-matched` (proposed)\n   - `:container-query-not-matched` (proposed)\n\n3. **Additional Features**\n   - View transition integration with container queries\n   - Container query-relative units (cqmin, cqmax)\n   - Optimal containment size determination\n\n## References\n\n- [CSS Containment Module Level Queries](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/Container_queries)\n\n## Conclusion\n\nCSS Container Queries are fully implemented in Servo's stylo styling engine, with comprehensive support
